content: Cleave extension fully implemented at extensions/cleave/ (index.ts + assessment.ts, conflicts.ts, dispatcher.ts, planner.ts, types.ts, workspace.ts, worktree.ts). Registered in package.json pi.extensions. Skills/prompts still exist as legacy.
project-memory episodic memory: session narratives generated at shutdown via generateEpisode subagent, stored with embeddings for semantic search
project-memory uses Ollama (qwen3-embedding) for semantic retrieval; falls back to FTS5 keyword search if unavailable. See extensions/project-memory/embeddings.ts
pi-kit is a pi-package (extensions, prompts, skills, themes) for the pi coding agent. Root: ~/workspace/ai/pi-kit, package.json has `"keywords": ["pi-package"]`.

project-memory extension: SQLite+WAL storage (.pi/memory/facts.db), semantic retrieval via Ollama qwen3-embedding, working memory buffer (25-slot cap), episodic session narratives, background extraction via subagent, global knowledge base (~/.pi/memory/global.db), JSONL export/import for git sync, context pressure system with auto-compaction.

Cleave OpenSpec integration at extensions/cleave/openspec.ts: detectOpenSpec, listChanges, findExecutableChanges, parseTasksFile, taskGroupsToChildPlans, openspecChangeToSplitPlan, openspecChangeToSplitPlanWithContext, buildOpenSpecContext, parseDesignFileChanges, parseDesignDecisions, readSpecScenarios, supplementScopeFromDesign. Tests at extensions/cleave/openspec.test.ts. Wired into /cleave as fast path.

OpenSpec (Fission-AI/OpenSpec) is a spec-driven development CLI: proposal→specs→design→tasks workflow with delta-spec merge on archive. Core profiles: /opsx:propose, /opsx:apply, /opsx:archive. Expanded: /opsx:ff, /opsx:continue, /opsx:verify, /opsx:sync. Cleave integrates as execution layer for tasks.md. Repo: https://github.com/Fission-AI/OpenSpec

pi core compaction exposes session_before_compact hook — extension can provide custom compaction summary (extensionCompaction) by returning {compaction: {summary, firstKeptEntryId, tokensBefore, details}} from the event handler. See dist/core/agent-session.js:_runAutoCompaction()

pi-kit's only upstream dependency is `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` (the official extension API). All `@mariozechner/pi-ai` imports eliminated — StringEnum inlined to extensions/lib/typebox-helpers.ts. Runtime imports from pi-coding-agent limited to public exports: ExtensionAPI (type), DynamicBorder, serializeConversation, convertToLlm.

design-tree and cleave are bridged via design_tree_update 'implement' action: scaffolds openspec/changes/<node-id>/ from a decided node's content, then /cleave executes it. Full pipeline: design → decide → implement → /cleave → verify.

Design-tree extension refactored into 3 modules: types.ts (NodeStatus, DesignNode, DocumentSections), tree.ts (pure domain logic — parse/generate frontmatter+sections, scan, mutations, branching), index.ts (2 tools + commands + lifecycle). Agent can now autonomously create/mutate design nodes via design_tree_update tool. Documents have structured sections: Overview, Research, Decisions, Open Questions, Implementation Notes. Open questions synced between body and frontmatter. 'implement' action bridges decided nodes to OpenSpec by scaffolding proposal.md + design.md + tasks.md from the node's content. [2026-03-06]

OpenSpec extension at extensions/openspec/: types.ts (ChangeInfo, Scenario, Requirement, SpecFile, SpecSection, ChangeStage types), spec.ts (pure domain logic — getOpenSpecDir, ensureOpenSpecDir, listChanges, getChange, computeStage, parseSpecContent, parseScenarios, generateSpecFromProposal, formatScenario, generateSpecFile, createChange, addSpec, archiveChange, countScenarios, summarizeSpecs), index.ts (1 tool + 7 commands + message renderers). Registered in package.json pi.extensions.
defaults.ts content-hash guard: sha256 of last-deployed AGENTS.md stored at ~/.pi/agent/.agents-md-hash; mismatched hash triggers warning instead of overwrite. See extensions/defaults.ts.

Pi core model IDs use alias form without dates (e.g. 'claude-opus-4-6', not 'claude-opus-4-20250514'). Alias preferred per model-resolver.js comment. Bedrock uses 'us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-6-v1'. See dist/core/model-resolver.js.

docs/img/spec-pipeline.png: new D2 diagram showing design→propose→spec→contract→plan→implement→verify→archive pipeline with feedback loop. docs/img/architecture.png: regenerated with all current extension groups.

README.md updated: reflects current extensions with architecture/spec-pipeline diagrams. spec-pipeline.png added at docs/img/spec-pipeline.png (commit 73728d0). README references diagram inline instead of text pipeline description.

openspec/, design/, and .github/workflows/ directories initialized in pi-kit. openspec has baseline/, changes/, and README.md; design/ has README.md; CI workflow runs npm test on Node 20+22 on push/PR to main.
OpenSpecContext includes `apiContract: string | null`; buildOpenSpecContext checks api.yaml, api.yml, api.json (in that order, first found wins). See extensions/cleave/openspec.ts.
openspec/archive/.gitkeep added so archive directory is tracked in git. openspec/ structure: baseline/, changes/, archive/, README.md.

TaskGroup.specDomains: string[] added to openspec.ts; parseTasksFile parses <!-- specs: domain/name, ... --> annotations on lines after group headers (only before first task). ChildPlan.specDomains? also added in types.ts. See extensions/cleave/openspec.ts, extensions/cleave/types.ts.

ChildPlanWithDomains interface removed from workspace.ts — specDomains now lives directly on ChildPlan (types.ts). matchScenariosToChildren operates on ChildPlan.specDomains?. See extensions/cleave/workspace.ts, extensions/cleave/types.ts.

matchScenariosToChildren returns Map<number, AssignedScenario[]> keyed by child index; every spec scenario guaranteed to appear in at least one child (orphan safety net). See extensions/cleave/workspace.ts.

version-check extension implemented at extensions/version-check.ts: polls GitHub releases API hourly via setInterval, compares semver against package.json version, sends sendMessage notification on new release. Respects PI_SKIP_VERSION_CHECK and PI_OFFLINE env vars. Registered in package.json pi.extensions.
version-check.test.ts lives at extensions/version-check.test.ts (root level, not a subdir); requires explicit `extensions/*.test.ts` glob to be picked up by the test runner.

pi package-manager installGit supports `source.ref` for checkout on fresh clone, but updateGit always resets to @{upstream} branch — no tag-tracking on update. Tag-pinned installs via parseGitUrl (see dist/utils/git.js).

Cleave child processes are spawned as `pi -p --no-session` subprocesses with the prompt via stdin, working dir set to the worktree, and PI_CHILD=1 env var. They run a full pi agent with all extensions loaded. See extensions/cleave/dispatcher.ts.

pi child processes support --model <id> or --model <provider>/<pattern> CLI flag with fuzzy matching via resolveCliModel() in dist/core/model-resolver.js — enables tier routing by passing model IDs to spawnChild().

buildChildPrompt() in dispatcher.ts extended design: skillPaths param adds 'Specialist Skills' directive with explicit read instructions per matched skill file; tiered execution loop wraps spawnChild with review phase using opus model via --model flag.

Pi TUI design investigation initiated: exploring pi-coding-agent TUI hooks for a live sidebar showing cleave/design-tree/openspec state. See /opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/docs/tui.md and extensions.md.
Pi TUI overlays support real-time animation (~30 FPS) via tui.setOverlay() OverlayHandle pattern; components call tui.invalidate() to trigger redraws. Enables live-updating status panels.

Pi TUI overlay system supports persistent side-panels via ctx.ui.setOverlay() with anchor/width/maxHeight options; responsive hiding when terminal < 100 cols. See /opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/examples/extensions/overlay-qa-tests.ts command /overlay-sidepanel.
Pi overlay custom() components get OverlayHandle for external visibility control via onHandle:(handle)=>{...} option in custom() call; handle stored in outer scope for event-driven show/hide.

Pi overlay system persistent sidepanel pattern: ctx.ui.custom() with {overlay:true, overlayOptions:{anchor:'right-center', width:'25%', minWidth:30, visible:(termWidth)=>termWidth>=100}}; OverlayHandle.setHidden() toggled via onHandle callback. See /opt/homebrew/.../overlay-qa-tests.ts /overlay-sidepanel.

pi-tui OverlayHandle interface: hide() permanently removes overlay, setHidden(bool) temporarily shows/hides, isHidden() checks state. Lives at /opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-tui/dist/tui.d.ts
pi-tui OverlayOptions: width/maxHeight as SizeValue (number or '25%'), anchor, offsetX/Y, row/col, margin (OverlayMargin|number), visible:(termWidth,termHeight)=>boolean. See tui.d.ts lines ~75-103.

ctx.ui.setWidget() accepts either string[] or (tui,theme)=>Component factory — only non-blocking persistent view mechanism in pi. Multiple widgets stack with placement option (belowEditor). See extensions/types.d.ts.
ctx.ui.setFooter(factory) replaces entire footer; factory gets FooterDataProvider with git branch + extension statuses + onBranchChange() callback. Available for dashboard use.
pi overlay custom() is always blocking (await ctx.ui.custom()) — keyboard input goes to overlay not editor. No truly persistent non-blocking sidepanel exists within pi's TUI model.

design node 'unified-dashboard' created at docs/unified-dashboard.md: exploring unified live dashboard for cleave+design-tree+openspec with 3 research sections (TUI API audit, current fragmented state, 4 architecture options A-D).
openspec/changes/skill-aware-dispatch/ scaffolded: proposal.md, design.md, tasks.md (3 groups, 31 tasks), specs/cleave/spec.md (7 requirements, 20 scenarios). See that directory.

extensions/cleave/review.ts implemented (518 lines): ReviewVerdict, ReviewIssue, ReviewConfig, ReviewRound, buildReviewPrompt, buildFixPrompt, parseReviewResult, severityGate, detectChurn, executeWithReview — full adversarial review loop. Tests at review.test.ts (545 lines).

extensions/cleave/skills.ts implemented (343 lines): SkillMapping interface, DEFAULT_MAPPINGS (python/rust/OCI/etc.), matchSkillsToChild(), resolveSkillPaths(), globMatches(). Tests at skills.test.ts (345 lines).

Cleave skill-aware dispatch fully merged to main: skills.ts + review.ts + dispatcher.ts + index.ts updated; 3 child branches (skill-matching, prompt-and-routing, review-loop) merged with conflict resolution. 2545 lines added across 12 files.
ChildPlan fields added: skills: string[] (skill matching), executeModel?: ModelTier (tier routing), specDomains: string[] (required, defaults []). See extensions/cleave/types.ts.

Cleave skill matching at extensions/cleave/skills.ts: SkillMapping interface, DEFAULT_MAPPINGS (python, rust, oci, k8s-operations, typescript, style, git), matchSkillsToChild (annotation-first then auto-match from scope globs), resolveSkillPaths, resolveModelTier. Tests at skills.test.ts. [2026-03-07]

Cleave review loop at extensions/cleave/review.ts: executeWithReview wraps child dispatch in adversarial review cycle. ReviewExecutor interface for testability. buildReviewPrompt (hostile reviewer), parseReviewResult (regex-based C/W/N extraction), severityGate (nits→accept, warnings→1 fix, critical→2 fixes, security→immediate escalate), detectChurn (normalized description comparison, Jaccard >50% threshold). ReviewConfig controls enabled/maxWarningFixes/maxCriticalFixes/churnThreshold. Tests at review.test.ts. [2026-03-07]

pi interactive mode maintains two widget containers (widgetContainerAbove, widgetContainerBelow) as Maps keyed by extension key; multiple extensions can each setWidget() independently — they stack vertically within the same container. renderWidgets() rebuilds container children on each update. See interactive-mode.js lines ~115-947.

pi footer: FooterDataProvider exposes getGitBranch(), getExtensionStatuses(), getAvailableProviderCount(), onBranchChange(). setFooter() replaces built-in FooterComponent entirely. Only one custom footer at a time (customFooter field). ReadonlyFooterDataProvider is a Pick of the non-mutating methods. See footer-data-provider.d.ts.

pi layout order (top→bottom): widgetContainerAbove → conversation area → widgetContainerBelow → footer. setWidget with placement='belowEditor' slots into widgetContainerBelow. Footer is always the last child of ui.

pi.events (EventBus) is accessible on the ExtensionAPI: emit(channel, data) and on(channel, handler)=>unsubscribe. Can be used for cross-extension pub/sub without shared-state coupling. See dist/core/event-bus.d.ts.

pi TUI layout order (top→bottom): headerContainer → chatContainer → widgetContainerAbove → editorContainer → widgetContainerBelow → footer. widgetContainerAbove gets a leading spacer when non-empty; MAX_WIDGET_LINES=10.

Cleave dispatcher.ts integrates ReviewConfig via dispatchChildren/dispatchSingleChild; review loop is off by default (DEFAULT_REVIEW_CONFIG.enabled=false); wired into executeWithReview per child. See extensions/cleave/dispatcher.ts.

pi footer can render 2-3 lines (pwd, stats, optional extension statuses). If extension statuses are registered via setStatus(), they appear on a 3rd line sorted alphabetically. setFooter() replaces all 3 lines and must reimplement ~60 lines of pwd/token/model/context% logic.
unified-dashboard OpenSpec change scaffolded at openspec/changes/unified-dashboard/ (proposal.md, design.md, tasks.md). Status: decided, ready for spec writing and /cleave execution.

Unified live-view dashboard extension will live at extensions/dashboard/ (not extensions/view/). Design node at docs/unified-dashboard.md. Status: decided, being implemented via OpenSpec change at openspec/changes/unified-dashboard/.

design-tree extension emitter changes merged: updateWidget replaced with emitDesignTreeState writing sharedState.designTree; widget subcommand and widgetHidden removed. See extensions/design-tree/index.ts.

SharedState in extensions/shared-state.ts extended with designTree?, openspec?, cleave? properties; imports types from ./dashboard/types.ts; exports DASHBOARD_UPDATE_EVENT = 'dashboard:update'. See extensions/shared-state.ts.
openspec extension emitter changes merged: emitOpenSpecState added writing sharedState.openspec after session_start and after change mutations. See extensions/openspec/index.ts.

extensions/dashboard/types.ts created with: DesignTreeDashboardState, DesignTreeFocusedNode, OpenSpecDashboardState, OpenSpecChangeEntry, CleaveState, CleaveChildState, CleaveStatus, DashboardMode, FooterState interfaces.

cleave extension emitter changes merged: emitCleaveState added in cleave/index.ts for lifecycle transitions; dispatcher.ts mirrors child state to sharedState.cleave.children. See extensions/cleave/index.ts, extensions/cleave/dispatcher.ts.

setExtensionFooter in interactive-mode.js: removes built-in footer from ui, calls factory(tui, theme, footerDataProvider), adds result as child. Restoring to null restores built-in footer. footerDataProvider is the same ReadonlyFooterDataProvider passed to setFooter factory.

ctx.model is Model<any>|undefined with fields: id, name, provider, reasoning, contextWindow, maxTokens, cost. ctx.sessionManager.state.thinkingLevel available via session-manager.d.ts. See /opt/homebrew/.../pi-coding-agent/node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-ai/dist/types.d.ts
DashboardFooter.setContext(ctx) must be called on each event to keep token stats/model/context-usage current — ctx is not available at footer construction time. See extensions/dashboard/footer.ts.
unified-dashboard OpenSpec change archived (2026-03-07). Specs merged to baseline/: shared-state.md, emitters.md, footer.md, integration.md. Change at archive/2026-03-07-unified-dashboard.

extensions/dashboard/ implementation complete and smoke-tested: index.ts (setFooter + ctrl+shift+d + /dashboard), footer.ts (DashboardFooter, compact 3-line + raised 9-line modes, context gauge, token stats, model+thinking), overlay.ts (stub), types.ts. Renders correctly via jiti. Registered in package.json. status-bar.ts removed.

bootstrap.sh capability matrix updated: OpenSpec added as always-ready; Cleave shows 'partial' when no local models (qwen3/nemotron/devstral) are detected, 'ready' when local models available, 'missing' when git/node absent. See bootstrap.sh.

DashboardOverlay item navigation: flat ListItem[] with depth/expandable/expandedKeys for hierarchical display; rebuildItems() on tab switch or expand/collapse; render() outputs bordered panel with tab bar + content + key hint footer. Progress bars rendered inline on expand for OpenSpec changes.
extensions/view/index.ts scaffolded but superseded by extensions/dashboard/ — can be removed.

Component interface in pi-tui requires: render(width:number):string[], invalidate():void. Optional: handleInput?(data:string):void, wantsKeyRelease?:boolean. No dispose() in Component itself — dispose is in the return type of custom()/setFooter()/setWidget() factory signatures. See tui.d.ts lines ~9-30.

ctx.ui.custom<T>() factory signature: (tui, theme, keybindings:KeybindingsManager, done:(result:T)=>void) => (Component & {dispose?():void}) | Promise<...>. Options: overlay?, overlayOptions?, onHandle?. Returns Promise<T>. See extensions/types.d.ts.

showExtensionCustom (interactive-mode.js:1335) implements ctx.ui.custom(): saves editor text, creates overlay or replaces editor, resolves Promise when done(result) called. Overlay path calls this.ui.showOverlay(component) and sets focus; close() calls this.ui.hideOverlay(). Shortcut handler runs async via Promise.resolve, no streaming guard.
truncateToWidth(text, maxWidth, ellipsis?, pad?) in pi-tui/dist/utils.d.ts — pad=true pads to exactly maxWidth with spaces. ANSI-aware via visibleWidth. Exported from @mariozechner/pi-tui index.

DashboardOverlay.setEventBus() wires live refresh from DASHBOARD_UPDATE_EVENT; showDashboardOverlay now accepts optional pi param and passes pi.events to overlay; dispose() unsubscribes. See extensions/dashboard/overlay.ts.

DashboardOverlay.setEventBus() wires DASHBOARD_UPDATE_EVENT for live refresh while overlay is open; dispose() unsubscribes (no eventBus field retained after unsubscribe). MAX_CONTENT_LINES=30 caps content before footer rows. See extensions/dashboard/overlay.ts.

DashboardFooter incremental scan fixed: cachedThinkingLevel updated in same forward pass as token stats via thinking_level_change entry check — eliminates O(n) reverse scan. See extensions/dashboard/footer.ts.

extensions/dashboard/overlay-data.ts: pure data builders (buildDesignItems, buildOpenSpecItems, buildCleaveItems, rebuildItems, clampIndex, statusIcon, TABS, MAX_CONTENT_LINES, ThemeFn) extracted from overlay.ts. No pi-tui dependency — fully testable. Tests at extensions/dashboard/overlay-data.test.ts (27 tests).

extensions/dashboard/overlay.ts fully implemented: DashboardOverlay class with 3-tab interface (Design Tree/OpenSpec/Cleave), keyboard navigation (↑↓/Tab/1-3/←→/↵/Esc), progress bars, expand/collapse. Rendering logic imports pure builders from overlay-data.ts. showDashboardOverlay() anchors right-center 40% width.

@mariozechner/pi-tui resolves at /opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-tui — nested dep, not globally installed. Other extensions (design-tree, 01-auth, openspec) also import value symbols from pi-tui and work via pi's jiti loader.

pi extension jiti aliases (Node.js mode): @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent, @mariozechner/pi-tui, @mariozechner/pi-agent-core, @mariozechner/pi-ai, @mariozechner/pi-ai/oauth, @sinclair/typebox. Set in getAliases() at dist/core/extensions/loader.js:56.

Design tree lifecycle design node at docs/design-tree-lifecycle.md: exploring multi-branch `implementing` state with `branches: string[]` frontmatter, D1-D2-D4-D5 decided. Q3 (acceptance gate + branch association) still open.

docs.styrene.io is an Astro 5 static site (~/workspace/styrene-lab/styrene/site/) with custom remark-wikilinks plugin, pagefind search, force-graph local graph, and nginx serving. Deps: astro, @astrojs/sitemap, pagefind, unist-util-visit. No Starlight. See ~/workspace/styrene-lab/styrene/site/.

styrene remark-wikilinks plugin at ~/workspace/styrene-lab/styrene/site/src/plugins/remark-wikilinks.ts: builds slug→URL map from filesystem at init time, supports [[target]] and [[target|Display Text]], unresolved links render as styled concept refs. Configurable via slugMap or siteRoot scan.
styrene LocalGraph.astro: 1-hop neighborhood graph via force-graph CDN library, no client-side framework dependency. graphJson+currentId props passed at build time. ~132 lines.

Proposed obsidian/vault extension scope: (1) skill teaching agent to emit [[wikilinks]] and frontmatter in generated markdown; (2) extension /vault init (scaffold minimal Astro config) + /vault serve (run astro dev hot-reload) pointed at .pi/ + openspec/. Write-only agent→human viewport.

extensions/openspec/archive-gate.ts: transitionDesignNodesOnArchive(cwd, changeName) scans design tree for nodes with matching openspec_change field, transitions implementing→implemented, returns transitioned node IDs. Wired into openspec/index.ts archive case.

Design tree lifecycle OpenSpec change at openspec/changes/design-tree-lifecycle/: types.ts (implementing/implemented NodeStatus + branches[]/openspec_change on DesignNode), tree.ts (parse/serialize new frontmatter, matchBranchToNode, appendBranch), index.ts (before_agent_start branch polling, archive gate hook), dashboard updates (implementingCount/implementedCount/implementingNodes). archive-gate.ts cross-extension module. Tasks 1-5 complete except 2.5 unit tests.

design-tree implement action (index.ts): auto-transitions to 'implementing', creates feature/<node-id> branch via execSync git checkout -b, writes openspec_change (=node ID) and branches[] frontmatter, calls appendBranch. Non-fatal if branch exists. Optional branch prefix parameter supported.

Design tree node lifecycle: `seed → exploring → decided → implementing → implemented` with git branch binding. implement action scaffolds OpenSpec change, creates branch, sets implementing. OpenSpec archive gate transitions implementing→implemented via transitionDesignNodesOnArchive(). See extensions/design-tree/index.ts and extensions/openspec/archive-gate.ts.

Design tree lifecycle extended: seed → exploring → decided → implementing → implemented. `implement` action auto-creates feature/<node-id> branch, sets status to implementing, writes openspec_change and branches[] to frontmatter. Branch auto-association via before_agent_start polling with segment-aware matchBranchToNode (longest match wins). OpenSpec archive triggers implementing→implemented transition via archive-gate.ts. Dashboard shows ⚙ icon for implementing, ✓ for implemented. [2026-03-07]

mdserve fork at ~/workspace/ai/mdserve (cwilson613/mdserve on GitHub): Rust/Axum markdown preview server, upstream jfernandez/mdserve. 1857 lines in src/app.rs + src/main.rs. Key structs: MarkdownState, TrackedFile. Features: directory scan, live reload via WebSocket, Mermaid diagrams, minijinja templates.

Styrene brand tokens source of truth: ~/workspace/styrene-lab/styrene/site/src/styles/tokens.css. Key colors: --primary #00f0d3, --bg #16171d, --fg #cbf4ed, --bg-card #202b30, --border #4a6767. Noosphere src/app.rs references same palette as egui Color32 constants.

noosphere project at github.com/cwilson613/noosphere: wgpu/eframe (egui) 3D semantic zoom canvas backed by Loro CRDT. Rust, ~9k lines. Uses Styrene brand palette (tokens from brand_tokens.json). No web rendering — pure egui. No wikilinks. See /tmp/noosphere or clone fresh.

mdserve fork at ~/workspace/ai/mdserve: single minijinja template (templates/main.html) with all CSS inline, 5 themes via CSS custom props, sidebar nav, WebSocket live reload, Mermaid support. Adding: recursive dir scan, wikilink resolution (src/wikilinks.rs), Styrene theme, graph view (src/graph.rs + /graph endpoint).

mdserve rebuild_all() pattern: slug_map rebuilt from all keys → all files re-rendered with wikilinks → all edges accumulated → graph rebuilt. Called on init, file add, and file change. See src/app.rs MarkdownState::rebuild_all().

mdserve fork at ~/workspace/ai/mdserve: src/wikilinks.rs (build_slug_map with full+filename slug variants, resolve_wikilinks with WikilinkEdge, regex LazyLock, unresolved→concept-ref span), src/graph.rs (build_graph over tracked file keys + edges, local_graph 1-hop neighborhood, GraphNode/GraphEdge/GraphData, Serialize). regex = "1" in Cargo.toml. Branch: feature/wikilinks-graph. (2026-03-07)

mdserve handle_markdown_file_change fixed: uses MarkdownState::path_to_key() with base_dir for key lookup instead of bare filename. Watcher now uses RecursiveMode::Recursive. Graph routes added: /graph.json (serve_graph_json) and /graph (serve_graph_page with force-graph CDN). See src/app.rs.

mdserve templates/main.html: Styrene theme added (#16171d bg, #cbf4ed text, #00f0d3 link, #4a6767 border), theme card CSS + modal entry added, wikilink/.concept-ref CSS styles added. Graph page uses force-graph@1.49.0 CDN with Styrene/Catppuccin/dark/light theme support via CSS custom props.

design-tree executeImplement() shared function: validates scaffold result, sanitizes branch name, performs status transition + openspec_change write + git branch creation atomically. Both tool handler and /design command delegate to it. See extensions/design-tree/index.ts.

mdserve fork at ~/workspace/ai/mdserve (cwilson613/mdserve on GitHub): Rust single-binary markdown preview server forked from jfernandez/mdserve. Added: recursive dir scanning, [[wikilink]] resolution (wikilinks.rs), graph data + force-graph.js visualization (graph.rs, /graph endpoint), Styrene theme. 5.8MB release binary. Branch: feature/wikilinks-graph.

mdserve app.rs: scan_markdown_files() recursive (skips hidden dirs, node_modules, target); file keys are base_dir-relative paths (e.g., 'docs/vision.md'); watcher uses RecursiveMode::Recursive; MarkdownState gains slug_map, graph_data, all_edges fields; rebuild_all() centralizes wikilink+graph refresh. Routes: GET /, /ws, /mermaid.min.js, /graph.json, /graph, /*filename. wikilink edge targets use URL-resolved canonical slug (not raw wikilink text). See src/app.rs.

mdserve wikilinks.rs edge target fix: WikilinkEdge.target is derived from the resolved URL (strip leading / and .md), not the raw wikilink slug — ensures edges use canonical 'docs/vision' form matching node IDs. Committed 8bb9e8b on feature/wikilinks-graph.

mdserve localStorage keys are project-scoped via `window._lsKey(k)` helper (base_dir path prefix): `<base_dir>:theme`, `<base_dir>:sidebar-collapsed`. Applied to both main view and graph page. See templates/main.html + src/app.rs serve_graph_page(). Committed bb5da97.

vault extension at extensions/vault/index.ts: /vault [stop|status|graph] commands spawn mdserve binary (hasCmd guard). /vault serves project root at port 3333, opens browser, cleans up on session_end. skills/vault/SKILL.md teaches wikilink conventions and mdserve usage.
extensions/vault/index.ts: /vault command spawns mdserve binary (hasCmd check), subcommands: start (default), stop, status, graph. Registered in package.json pi.extensions. Cleans up on session_end.
skills/vault/SKILL.md: wikilink conventions ([[target]], [[target|display]]), frontmatter fields, graph-friendly patterns, mdserve CLI usage, and installation instructions from cwilson613/mdserve.
prompts/ directory may not exist in pi-kit — checked during /init prompt design investigation. Structure unknown.

pi-kit prompts/ directory contains markdown files with YAML frontmatter (description field) + body instructions. Prompts are registered via package.json pi.prompts=["./prompts"]. Examples: prompts/new-repo.md, prompts/oci-login.md.

/exit command in project-memory/index.ts: runs final extraction cycle, shows fact count delta notification, then calls ctx.shutdown() which triggers session_shutdown (episode generation + JSONL export). sharedState already imported; plan to add session summary display using sharedState (design tree, openspec) + git before shutdown.

vault extension simplified: hasBinary() now delegates to DEPS.find('mdserve').check() from bootstrap/deps.ts. /vault install subcommand removed. Points to /bootstrap when mdserve missing. See extensions/vault/index.ts.

extensions/bootstrap/deps.test.ts: 7 tests covering registry integrity (unique IDs, install commands, purpose/usedBy, checkAll count, tier validity, formatReport markdown output, core deps presence). All pass under tsx.

mdserve deps.ts registry: hasBinary() in vault/index.ts delegates to DEPS.find('mdserve').check() — returns false (not undefined) when dep not found due to optional chaining `dep ? dep.check() : false`. See extensions/vault/index.ts:hasBinary().

bootstrap dep registry (extensions/bootstrap/deps.ts): Dep interface has InstallOption[] (platform: darwin|linux|any), requires?: string[] fields. Exports: bestInstallCmd(), installHints(), sortByRequires(), checkAll(), formatReport(). cargo moved to recommended tier (required by mdserve). See extensions/bootstrap/deps.ts.

bootstrap extension at extensions/bootstrap/: deps.ts (declarative dep registry with platform-aware InstallOption[], requires[] prereq ordering) + index.ts (async install via spawn not execSync, sortByRequires for prereq ordering, 5min timeout, Linux install hints). MARKER_VERSION='1'. See extensions/bootstrap/.

pi-kit has a shared debug logger at `extensions/debug.ts` — controlled by `PI_DEBUG` env var (PI_DEBUG=1 for all, PI_DEBUG=dashboard,openspec for specific scopes). Outputs timestamped JSON to stderr. Instrumented in: openspec, cleave, design-tree, dashboard (index + footer).
pi's Shift+Ctrl+D debug handler (handleDebugCommand) writes a snapshot log to ~/.pi/agent/pi-debug.log — it is NOT a live panel and has no API for extensions to add debug entries to it.

smart-tool-profiles design node created at docs/smart-tool-profiles.md: exploring smart tool/skill activation by project context — tools can be toggled via pi.setActiveTools(), skills self-select via globs, extensions always load. Key insight: tool activation is the primary runtime lever.

pi-kit tool inventory: ~30 pi-kit tools + ~30 MCP scribe tools ≈ 60 tools in context window per session. ~12K tokens of tool descriptions alone (60 tools × ~200 tokens each). See docs/smart-tool-profiles.md for full groupings.

tool-profile extension (extensions/tool-profile/): auto-detects project context at session_start and activates only relevant tool profiles via pi.setActiveTools(). Profiles: core (always), coding (.git), visual (.excalidraw/d2), local-ai (ollama), web (always), scribe (mcp.json), pi-dev (pi.extensions in package.json). manage_tools LLM tool for agent self-serve. /profile command for user override. .pi/profile.json for declarative config.

project-memory generateEpisodeDirect() in extraction-v2.ts: calls Ollama /api/chat directly via fetch(), no subprocess spawn. ~500ms vs ~3s subprocess path. Used by /exit handler and as fast-path in session_shutdown. See extensions/project-memory/extraction-v2.ts

DashboardFooter.renderFooterData() fully reimplements pi's default footer: pwd+branch line (process.cwd()+footerData.getGitBranch()), stats line (token counts+cost+context%), and extension-status line (footerData.getExtensionStatuses()). Pi's built-in FooterComponent is entirely replaced. All visible footer lines are pi-kit's own rendering.

OpenSpecChangeEntry in dashboard/types.ts extended with artifacts?(proposal|design|specs|tasks) and specDomains?:string[] fields; emitOpenSpecState() in openspec/index.ts now populates both from ChangeInfo.hasProposal/hasDesign/hasSpecs/hasTasks and specs[].domain. loadExtensionModule in pi loader.js creates a new jiti instance per extension with moduleCache:false via createJiti(import.meta.url,...). jiti is @mariozechner/jiti at /opt/homebrew/.../pi-coding-agent/node_modules/@mariozechner/jiti/dist/jiti.cjs (minified single-line). See dist/core/extensions/loader.js:217-226.

pi's jiti loader uses filesystem cache at $(tmpdir)/jiti/ with content-hash filenames (e.g. dashboard-footer.dbb2aa84.mjs) — hash is content-based so new content produces new cache file, but old cache files accumulate and are NOT auto-cleaned.

/refresh command added to extensions/bootstrap/index.ts: clears jiti fs cache at tmpdir()/jiti/ (rmSync recursive), then calls ctx.reload(). Uses pi.registerCommand('refresh', {handler}) with correct ExtensionCommandContext.reload() API. Committed 683426e.

pi-kit has no local node_modules by default — all deps resolve at runtime via pi's global jiti loader. npm install installs them locally (gitignored). node_modules now installed for typecheck purposes.

pi-kit tsconfig.json: module=ESNext, moduleResolution=Bundler, allowImportingTsExtensions=true, strict=true, noEmit=true, skipLibCheck=true, includes extensions/**/*.ts, excludes examples/. Scripts: `npm run typecheck` (tsc --noEmit), `npm run check` (tsc + tests). Baseline: 0 errors as of extension-type-safety cleave.

pi's read tool (dist/core/tools/read.js) calls resizeImage() from utils/image-resize.js before sending as base64 image attachment — images are conditionally resized before LLM sees them, but display in TUI may still be at terminal-width pixel density.

pi-tui renderImage() uses maxWidthCells param; Image component default is 60 cells. view extension renderer was using 120 — increased to 200 as new default. See extensions/view/index.ts message renderer.

guardrails.ts fully integrated across 5 points: I1 (workspace.ts child task injection via buildGuardrailSection), I2 (review.ts pre-check before opus, round 0 only), I3 (index.ts post-merge suite with 20-line capped output), I4 (index.ts /assess preamble), I6 (dashboard/index.ts non-blocking session-start health check). pi-coding-agent changelog sourced at https://raw.githubusercontent.com/badlogic/pi-mono/main/packages/coding-agent/CHANGELOG.md — check this URL for upstream API changes affecting pi-kit integrations.

pi 0.57.0 adds two major extension APIs: (1) before_provider_request hook — extensions can inspect or replace provider payloads before sending; (2) non-capturing overlays — OverlayOptions.nonCapturing + OverlayHandle.focus()/unfocus()/isFocused() for overlays that don't steal input from editor. See pi changelog and docs/extensions.md.

Non-capturing overlay API confirmed in pi 0.57.0: OverlayOptions.nonCapturing=true renders overlay without stealing keyboard focus; ctx.ui.custom() onHandle callback provides OverlayHandle for focus()/unfocus()/setHidden() control. For persistent non-modal panel: fire-and-forget (don't await) ctx.ui.custom(), store handle via onHandle. See /opt/homebrew/.../pi-tui/dist/tui.d.ts and docs/non-capturing-dashboard.md.

Non-capturing overlay API (pi 0.57.0): OverlayOptions.nonCapturing=true + OverlayHandle.focus()/unfocus()/isFocused() confirmed in tui.d.ts. ctx.ui.custom() onHandle callback provides handle after show. Fire-and-forget (don't await) pattern needed for persistent panel. See /opt/homebrew/.../pi-tui/dist/tui.d.ts.

Extension shortcut pipeline: CustomEditor.handleInput() checks onExtensionShortcut first before all other keybindings (including pi-tui debug key). This means pi-kit extension shortcuts should NOT be blocked by pi's internal handlers — Ctrl+Shift+B should fire if matchesKey() correctly parses it.

Kitty default keybindings consume most ctrl+shift+<letter> combos. Free letters in Kitty defaults: a, d, i, j, m, p, r, x, y. ctrl+shift+d still blocked by pi-tui debug handler. Best remaining candidates: ctrl+shift+i, ctrl+shift+m, ctrl+shift+p, ctrl+shift+r, ctrl+shift+x, ctrl+shift+y.

pi-tui overlay system composites overlays on top of content at viewport-fixed positions; main content always renders at full terminal width (this.terminal.columns). No mechanism exists for overlays to shrink the available width for main content — conversation content cannot reflow to respect overlay width. See /opt/homebrew/.../pi-tui/dist/tui.js:669.

ctrl+` keybind: backtick is in SYMBOL_KEYS in pi-tui keys.js so matchesKey() handles it; rawCtrlChar returns null for backtick (no legacy terminal escape); Kitty protocol sends CSI 96;5u. Kitty defaults have NO binding for ctrl+`.

Dashboard extension at extensions/dashboard/: index.ts (setFooter + ctrl+` + /dashboard [compact|raised|panel|focus|open]), footer.ts (compact/raised modes, /dashboard hint on summary line), overlay.ts (DashboardOverlay, both non-capturing persistent and legacy modal modes). Registered in package.json. 4-state cycle complete.

Dashboard Layer 2 overlay: DashboardOverlay class exported from overlay.ts. Two usage modes: (1) non-capturing persistent panel via fire-and-forget ctx.ui.custom() with nonCapturing:true (panel mode), (2) legacy modal via showDashboardOverlay() blocking ctx.ui.custom() (focused/open mode). Esc in non-capturing mode hides panel; Esc while focused unfocuses to panel mode.

DashboardFooter renderCompact() shows '/dashboard' hint (theme.fg('dim')) appended to the summary line for discoverability. Panel and focused modes render compact footer (detail shown in overlay). See extensions/dashboard/footer.ts.

pi keybindings: ctrl+l=selectModel, ctrl+p=cycleModelForward, shift+ctrl+p=cycleModelBackward, shift+tab=cycleThinkingLevel, ctrl+o=expandTools, ctrl+t=toggleThinking, shift+ctrl+d=debug(reserved by pi-tui). Dashboard uses ctrl+` (free in Kitty defaults, backtick in SYMBOL_KEYS).

Non-capturing overlay lifecycle in index.ts: overlayHandle (OverlayHandle), overlayDone (done() callback), overlayCreated (bool) module vars. showPanel() creates once via fire-and-forget custom(), hides/shows via setHidden(). hidePanel() unfocuses+hides. focusPanel() calls handle.focus(). Permanently closed on session_shutdown via handle.hide()+done().

Dashboard has 4 modes: compact (footer summary), raised (footer detail sections), panel (non-capturing overlay, visible but doesn't steal input), focused (interactive overlay with keyboard nav). Cycle via ctrl+` or /dashboard. Non-capturing overlay uses pi 0.57.0's OverlayOptions.nonCapturing + OverlayHandle.focus()/unfocus(). Overlays are viewport-pinned (composite on top), NOT content-reflowing — no split-pane support in pi-tui.

Design tree nodes are stored as markdown files under docs/ (not .pi/design/ or design-tree/nodes/) — read/written by design-tree extension in the running process. Data changes are live; code changes require restart.

project-memory extractionModel defaults to 'claude-sonnet-4-6' (not local) — every background fact extraction cycle spawns a full pi subprocess calling Sonnet. See extensions/project-memory/types.ts DEFAULT_CONFIG.extractionModel. HIGH cost driver.
generateEpisodeDirect already uses local Ollama (qwen3:30b) for episode generation — episodic memory at session shutdown is already cost-optimized.

cost-reduction design node created at docs/cost-reduction.md: M1 Max 64GB local inference offload strategy. 7 ranked token sinks identified: fact extraction (high), cleave children (high), tool bloat (medium), extended thinking discipline (medium), compaction (low-medium), review loop (variable). See docs/cost-reduction.md.

cost-reduction design node at docs/cost-reduction.md: status exploring, 3 research sections (token sinks, local capability assessment, reduction estimates) + 3 decisions (extractionModel local, cleave autoclassification no-fail-past, local-first compaction).

project-memory extraction-v2.ts: isLocalModel() checks model name (no 'claude-' prefix, no '/' separator); runExtractionDirect() hits Ollama /api/chat directly; runExtractionV2/runGlobalExtraction use direct path for local models, fall back to pi subprocess with cloud model on Ollama failure.

cleave dispatcher.ts: classifyByScope(scope) auto-classifies children by scope size (LOCAL_SCOPE_THRESHOLD=3, SONNET_SCOPE_THRESHOLD=8); test files (.test.ts/.spec.ts) excluded from effective count; resolveExecuteModel() updated with scope param — explicit annotation > scope autoclassification > preferLocal flag > skill hint > sonnet default. No-fail-past enforced structurally.

Cleave scope-based autoclassification in dispatcher.ts: classifyByScope() counts non-test files — ≤3→local, 4-8→sonnet, 9+→opus. NO-FAIL-PAST rule: once classified local, child never escalates to cloud. preferLocal=true caps all children to local. See extensions/cleave/dispatcher.ts.

terminal-title extension at extensions/terminal-title.ts: rich tab title updates reflecting agent state (idle ✦, thinking ◆, tool ⚙, cleave ⚡, dashboard 📋), turn index, tool chain (last 2), cleave progress from sharedState, dashboard mode from pi.events DASHBOARD_UPDATE_EVENT.

effort-tiers OpenSpec change at openspec/changes/effort-tiers/ with specs/effort.md (7 requirements, 23 scenarios), design.md and tasks.md (7 groups, 33 tasks). Committed 67ccf34. Cleave completed: extensions/effort/types.ts, tiers.ts, tiers.test.ts, index.ts, model-budget-cap.test.ts created; model-budget.ts and dispatcher.ts updated. All 4 children merged successfully. See extensions/effort/.
effort extension registered in package.json pi.extensions before model-budget (order matters: effort writes sharedState.effort in session_start, model-budget reads it). Commit 1b62eee.

project-memory config override pattern: session_start resets config to DEFAULT_CONFIG then applies sharedState.effort overrides (extractionModel for cloud tiers, compactionLocalFirst=false for non-local compaction). applyEffortToCfg() re-applies effort at runExtractionCycle call-time for mid-session /effort switches. See extensions/project-memory/index.ts.

project-memory compactionLocalFirst=true by default; session_before_compact reads sharedState.effort live at intercept time so mid-session /effort switches affect compaction routing. See extensions/project-memory/index.ts.

project-memory extractionModel switched to devstral-small-2:24b by default; direct Ollama HTTP path (runExtractionDirect) used for local models, falls back to cloud Sonnet only if Ollama unreachable. See extensions/project-memory/types.ts + extraction-v2.ts

applyEffortToCfg() helper in project-memory/index.ts re-applies sharedState.effort overrides to MemoryConfig at call-time — extraction model and compaction preference respond to mid-session /effort changes without restart.

Harness local model requirements: reliable structured JSON tool calls (24+ tools), multi-step orchestration, 16-32K context fidelity, instruction following, Rust+TS code quality. Even Qwen3 32B gives ~60-70% of Sonnet's capability on complex orchestration. Key failure modes: malformed tool call JSON, missed system-prompt directives, repetitive tool loops, slow inference (~10-15 tok/s at Q4 on 70B models).

Local Ollama models on M1 Max (64GB): current: nemotron-3-nano:30b, qwen3:30b, devstral-small-2:24b, qwen3-embedding:4b. Planned: qwen3:32b (~35GB Q8), qwen2.5-coder:32b (~35GB Q8), qwen2.5:72b (~48GB Q5_K_M). PREFERRED_ORDER auto-selects best installed model — no per-machine config needed.

devstral:24b is the current canonical Ollama tag (built on Mistral-Small-3.1, 53.6% SWE-Bench verified); devstral-small-2:24b is the previous tag. Both listed in KNOWN_MODELS; devstral:24b takes precedence in PREFERRED_ORDER_CODE.

offline-driver.ts imports KNOWN_MODELS, PREFERRED_ORDER, PREFERRED_ORDER_CODE, PREFERRED_FAMILIES from ./lib/local-models.ts and re-exports PREFERRED_ORDER/PREFERRED_ORDER_CODE for backward compatibility. Inline constants fully removed. Commit c43f75d.

project-memory compaction: compactionLocalFirst=true by default — session_before_compact intercepts and routes to direct Ollama HTTP (120s timeout, 60k char truncation, num_ctx=32768). Cloud only used when Ollama unreachable. Effort tier overrides at runtime via sharedState.effort.compaction. On cloud failure: onError increments compactionRetryCount, resets autoCompacted, next threshold trigger sets useLocalCompaction=true. Max 3 retry attempts. Resume relay via pi.sendMessage({display:false},{triggerTurn:true}). [2026-03-07]

Local model registry at extensions/lib/local-models.ts: single source of truth for KNOWN_MODELS (metadata), PREFERRED_ORDER (general orchestration, 70B→32B→MoE-30B→14B→8B→4B), PREFERRED_ORDER_CODE (code-biased for cleave leaf workers), PREFERRED_FAMILIES (prefix catch-alls for startsWith matching). All consumers (offline-driver, effort, cleave, project-memory) import from here. Update this file when new models release. [2026-03-07]

Effort-tiers system: 7-level global inference cost control (Servitor=0% cloud → Omnissiah=100%). extensions/effort/ — types.ts (EffortLevel, EffortConfig), tiers.ts (tierConfig() pure fn), index.ts (/effort command, /effort cap, session_start init). Cap derives ceiling from capLevel via tierConfig(), not effort.driver. Wired to: model-budget cap enforcement, project-memory extraction+compaction, cleave floor+preferLocal. sharedState.effort read live at decision points. [2026-03-07]

DashboardMode type: 'compact' | 'raised' | 'panel'. compact=footer only, raised=expanded footer, panel=non-capturing overlay with compact footer. 'focused' mode still exists internally via cycleTo but not in primary UX cycle. See extensions/dashboard/types.ts

pi-ai supports 'openai-codex' as a KnownProvider using ChatGPT OAuth (PKCE flow via openai-codex.js) — authenticates with ChatGPT Plus/Pro account, not API key. API: 'openai-codex-responses'. Available models: gpt-5.1-codex, gpt-5.1-codex-max, gpt-5.1-codex-mini, gpt-5.2-codex, gpt-5.3-codex, gpt-5.3-codex-spark, codex-mini-latest. See dist/providers/openai-codex-responses.js and dist/utils/oauth/openai-codex.js.

openai-codex provider uses WebSocket (wss://) or REST (/codex/responses) at DEFAULT_CODEX_BASE_URL. ChatGPT Plus/Pro OAuth grants access — no separate API key billing required. OAuth client_id: app_EMoamEEZ73f0CkXaXp7hrann, PKCE flow, redirect to localhost:1455.

pi-ai ships `openaiCodexOAuthProvider` as a built-in OAuth provider in `pi-ai/dist/utils/oauth/index.js` (alongside anthropic, github-copilot, gemini-cli, antigravity). Auth via `authStorage.login('openai-codex', callbacks)` — PKCE browser flow, no API key.

openai-codex models use `reasoning_effort` ('low'/'medium'/'high') not Anthropic's thinking token budget. `setThinkingLevel()` in model-budget.ts currently maps only to Anthropic's format — needs provider-aware handling.

gpt-5.3-codex-spark is $0/token (free under ChatGPT Pro) with 128K context and reasoning:true — zero-cost cloud reasoning model available via openai-codex provider, analogous to Tier 3-4 in effort-tiers.

pi model-resolver.js DEFAULT_MODELS map includes: openai→gpt-5.4, openai-codex→gpt-5.4, openrouter→openai/gpt-5.1-codex, github-copilot→gpt-4o. Built-in OAuth providers: anthropic, github-copilot, gemini-cli, antigravity, openai-codex (all in pi-ai/dist/utils/oauth/index.js BUILT_IN_OAUTH_PROVIDERS).
User is running pi-kit with gpt-5.4 (openai-codex provider) as an alternative to Claude Opus 4.6 — confirms openai-codex provider is functional in practice.
gpt-5.4 appears in model-resolver.js DEFAULT_MODELS for openai-codex provider; user is on GPT Pro OAuth with ChatGPT Pro. First confirmed use of openai-codex provider in pi-kit development context.

codex-tier-routing target: abstract tier labels (Servitor/Adept/Magos/Archmagos) map to per-provider model IDs at resolution time via new extensions/lib/model-routing.ts module. EffortConfig.driver will migrate from 'local'|'sonnet'|'opus' to these abstract labels.

Provider-aware model routing implemented in extensions/lib/model-routing.ts. resolveTier(tier, models, policy) maps abstract tiers (local|haiku|sonnet|opus) to concrete model IDs using session ProviderRoutingPolicy. Tier display labels: Servitor (local), Adept (haiku), Magos (sonnet), Archmagos (opus). OpenAI mapping: gpt-5.1-codex→haiku, gpt-5.3-codex-spark→sonnet, gpt-5.4→opus. Anthropic: prefix matching. extensions/lib/model-routing.ts: resolveTier(tier, models, policy?), getTierDisplayLabel(tier), getDefaultPolicy(), ProviderRoutingPolicy, ResolvedTierModel interfaces. dispatchChildren() preflight: checks LARGE_RUN_THRESHOLD (4) and requirePreflightForLargeRuns policy before dispatch; prompts operator via pi.ui.input() for provider preference; updates sharedState.routingPolicy.providerOrder if operator chooses. project-memory full-dump injection caps: 50 facts + 20 edges via renderForInjection(). Semantic mode injects all Constraints+Specs facts, pinned working-memory (cap 25), top 20 semantic hits, total capped at 30 project facts. Global mind: 15 facts, no edges. Recent episodes: up to 3. project-memory injection modes: (1) tiny — fact count <=3, inject welcome message only; (2) full — fallback when embeddings unavailable/coverage <50%/prompt short/facts <=20; (3) semantic — requires embeddings + >=50% vector coverage + prompt len >10 + facts >20. Mode determines composition structure.

OpenSpec change memory-mind-audit-instrumentation at openspec/changes/memory-mind-audit-instrumentation/: proposal.md, specs/memory.md (6 requirements, 7 scenarios), design.md, tasks.md. Goal: record MemoryInjectionSnapshot at generation time, expose via sharedState and /memory stats.

extensions/project-memory/injection-metrics.ts: MemoryInjectionMetrics interface with mode/projectFactCount/edgeCount/workingMemoryFactCount/semanticHitCount/episodeCount/globalFactCount/payloadChars/estimatedTokens; createMemoryInjectionMetrics(), estimateTokensFromChars(), formatMemoryInjectionMetrics() helpers. extensions/dashboard/file-watch.ts exports path filter helpers shouldRefreshDesignTreeForPath and shouldRefreshOpenSpecForPath; tests at extensions/dashboard/file-watch.test.ts (4 tests, all passing).

design-tree extension starts fs.watch() on docs/ directory (recursive) at session_start, debounced 75ms, calling scanDesignDocs + emitDesignTreeState on matching on-disk markdown changes. OpenSpec extension starts fs.watch() on openspec/ directory at session_start with same 75ms debounce pattern. Both use shouldRefreshDesignTreeForPath / shouldRefreshOpenSpecForPath guards from dashboard/file-watch.ts. Best-effort — unsupported platforms fall back to command/tool-driven emits.

extensions/dashboard/file-watch.ts: pure path-filter helpers shouldRefreshDesignTreeForPath(filePath, docsDir) and shouldRefreshOpenSpecForPath(filePath, repoRoot) for watcher guard logic. Tests at extensions/dashboard/file-watch.test.ts (4 tests). Imported by design-tree/index.ts and openspec/index.ts.

Dashboard live updates are now event-driven from both extension mutations AND direct disk edits. Design Tree and OpenSpec producers run fs.watch() on session_start, debounced 75ms, promoting filesystem changes into the dashboard:update event system. Watchers are best-effort; platforms that don't support recursive fs.watch fall back to mutation-driven updates only.

extensions/dashboard/ clickable links: Design Tree and OpenSpec items in footer and overlay now emit OSC 8 hyperlinks via uri-helper.ts; routes to mdserve for .md files, file:// as fallback. Wired in overlay-data.ts, footer.ts.

pi-kit version 0.3.1 released (2026-03-09): dashboard overlay openable row markers (↗), selection-prefers-first-openable, Enter opens non-expandable items, design-tree summary includes implemented/implementing counts. Commit 1fa2769, tag v0.3.1.
pi-kit version is 0.3.1 as of release tag v0.3.1 (commit 1fa2769). Previous: v0.3.0.

Driver model selection now persists in `.pi/config.json` as `lastUsedModel { provider, modelId }`; `extensions/model-budget.ts` writes it after successful tier switches, and `extensions/effort/index.ts` restores it on `session_start` before falling back to effort-tier resolution.

model-budget.ts exports buildSetModelTierDescription() and buildTierCommandDescription(tier) for provider-neutral UX copy; set_model_tier description updated to reference 'provider routing policy' and 'Anthropic, OpenAI, or local inference'. Tests at extensions/model-budget.test.ts.

effort/index.ts: restoreLastUsedModel() attempts to reload persisted model on session_start before falling back to tier's driver model. switchDriverModel() return type changed to RegistryModel|null and writes model on /effort switch via writeLastUsedModel().

extensions/lib/model-preferences.ts: readLastUsedModel(cwd)/writeLastUsedModel(cwd,{provider,modelId})/loadPiConfig(cwd) — persists last-used model to .pi/config.json under lastUsedModel key, preserving unrelated keys. Tests at extensions/lib/model-preferences.test.ts.
OpenSpec change 'provider-neutral-model-controls' archived (2026-03-09). Baseline at openspec/baseline/routing.md. Design node implemented at docs/provider-neutral-model-controls.md.

memory-lifecycle-integration design node at docs/memory-lifecycle-integration.md: status decided, implementing. D1: hybrid lifecycle-driven writes at 4 checkpoints (design decision, post-assess reconciliation, OpenSpec archive, bug fix resolution). D2: source precedence = OpenSpec baseline → Design Tree decided state → Memory → session chatter. D3: fact-first, graph edges optional in v1. OpenSpec change at openspec/changes/memory-lifecycle-integration/ with specs/memory/lifecycle.md (3 requirements, 9 scenarios).

memory-lifecycle-integration design node decided and implementing at docs/memory-lifecycle-integration.md: D1 hybrid lifecycle-driven writes, D2 source precedence (OpenSpec baseline→Design Tree→Memory→session chatter), D3 fact-first with optional graph edges in v1. OpenSpec change scaffolded at openspec/changes/memory-lifecycle-integration/.

openspec/changes/memory-lifecycle-integration/specs/memory/lifecycle.md: 3 requirements, 9 scenarios covering lifecycle candidate generation, confidence-gated auto-storage vs confirmation, and ephemeral chatter exclusion.

Lifecycle-driven memory integration uses a lightweight sharedState.lifecycleCandidateQueue for cross-extension coordination; design-tree, openspec, and cleave enqueue explicit candidates, and project-memory drains and ingests them on session_start/before_agent_start. See extensions/shared-state.ts and extensions/project-memory/index.ts.

project-memory now defaults to cloud-first memory models: extractionModel=`gpt-5.3-codex-spark`, embeddingProvider=`openai`, embeddingModel=`text-embedding-3-small`; semantic retrieval degrades to FTS5 when cloud embeddings are unavailable. See extensions/project-memory/types.ts and embeddings.ts

Slash-command bridging now lives in extensions/lib/slash-command-bridge.ts with allowlist metadata (`agentCallable`, sideEffectClass, requiresConfirmation, resultContract`) and a generic `execute_slash_command` tool wrapper; `/assess` in extensions/cleave/index.ts is registered through the bridge and exposes structured results for spec/diff/cleave/complexity while keeping bare/freeform `/assess` interactive-only in v1.

Startup model routing now sources sharedState.routingPolicy from the saved bootstrap operator profile during bootstrap session_start, before effort resolves its startup driver. See extensions/bootstrap/index.ts.
package.json extension order now loads offline-driver before effort so local driver models are registered before effort's session_start attempts driver resolution.

pi-kit already vendors a partial Excalidraw programmatic generation layer at extensions/render/excalidraw/ from @swiftlysingh/excalidraw-cli, but omits the upstream DSL parser, JSON parser, ELK layout, arrow router, and full generator pipeline. See extensions/render/excalidraw/UPSTREAM.md.

pi-kit now has a constrained Excalidraw composition layer at extensions/render/excalidraw/compose.ts that parses diagram specs, applies canonical layouts (pipeline/fanout/converge/grid), routes labeled edges, emits via the vendored Excalidraw factory layer, and validates documents before render/export.

Native document-bound diagram rendering now lives in extensions/render/native-diagrams/ with constrained motif parsing, deterministic motif compilation, a neutral scenegraph, direct SVG serialization, and browser-free PNG rasterization via resvg. The operator-facing tool is render_native_diagram in extensions/render/index.ts.

Persisted operator profiles now bridge into resolver-native CapabilityProfile/CapabilityRuntimeState structures via extensions/lib/operator-profile.ts, and effort/model-budget pass those structures into resolveTier().

Bootstrap now uses the canonical operator profile schema from extensions/lib/operator-profile.ts instead of a separate legacy bootstrap-only profile shape; it synthesizes defaults by reordering role candidates and derives routing policy from candidate ordering plus local-fallback posture.

OpenSpec/design-tree binding truth is centralized in `extensions/openspec/archive-gate.ts` via `resolveNodeOpenSpecBinding()` and `resolveBoundDesignNodes()`, which recognize both explicit `openspec_change` bindings and fallback node-id matches against known OpenSpec changes.

Bridged `/assess spec` now completes in-band by running a read-only child `pi --mode json --plan -p --no-session` subprocess from `extensions/cleave/index.ts`; interactive `/assess spec` remains follow-up-driven.

Cleave now runs a dirty-tree preflight before worktree creation: extensions/cleave/index.ts uses inspectGitState() plus workspace classification to summarize related, unrelated/unknown, and volatile paths and gate cleave on explicit operator action.

model-budget recovery now detects retryable structured provider envelopes that pi core's textual auto-retry regex misses (for example Codex JSON `server_error`) and schedules one extension-driven same-message retry, bounded by a per-request fingerprint ledger. See extensions/model-budget.ts.

cleave per-child progress updates now emit `dashboard:update` from extensions/cleave/dispatcher.ts when a child flips to running or done/failed, so terminal-title and dashboard consumers refresh live 0/N and done/N counts from sharedState.cleave.

All pi-kit slash commands are now registered with a shared SlashCommandBridge singleton (getSharedBridge() in extensions/lib/slash-command-bridge.ts, keyed by Symbol.for global). 7 OpenSpec commands + /assess are agent-callable; /dashboard and /dash are bridged with agentCallable:false for structured refusals. The agent can invoke any bridged command via execute_slash_command.

Dashboard extension provides compact/raised/panel/focused footer modes with live state from all subsystems. Decentralized emitters write to shared state; dashboard re-renders on DASHBOARD_UPDATE_EVENT. Terminal title mirrors compact state. Key files: extensions/dashboard/. See docs/dashboard.md

Cleave is the parallel execution engine: splits complex work into child tasks in isolated git worktrees, with skill-aware dispatch, adversarial review loop, and OpenSpec integration. /cleave command and /assess bridge. Key files: extensions/cleave/. See docs/cleave.md

Model routing manages all inference decisions: effort tiers (Servitor→Omnissiah), provider-aware resolution (Anthropic/OpenAI/Ollama), fallback with retry budgeting, and compaction model selection. Key files: extensions/model-budget.ts, extensions/lib/model-routing.ts, extensions/effort/. See docs/model-routing.md

Error recovery classifies upstream failures (context-overflow, auth, quota, rate-limit, image-too-large, invalid-request, retryable-flake) and applies bounded retry (max 1), provider failover, or operator notification. Request fingerprinting prevents loops. Key files: extensions/model-budget.ts, extensions/lib/model-routing.ts. See docs/error-recovery.md

Operator profile captures provider preferences and fallback policy. Bootstrap probes dependencies and provider readiness. Profile gates fallback — unapproved providers don't receive failover traffic. Key files: extensions/bootstrap/, extensions/lib/operator-profile.ts. See docs/operator-profile.md

Design tree manages design exploration lifecycle: seed→exploring→decided→implementing→implemented. Two tools (design_tree, design_tree_update) provide agent access. 'implement' action bridges to OpenSpec. Docs in docs/design/ (archived) and docs/ (active). Key files: extensions/design-tree/. See docs/design-tree.md

OpenSpec manages spec-driven development: propose→spec→fast-forward→cleave→assess spec→archive. Specs define Given/When/Then behavioral contracts. Delta-merge on archive to openspec/baseline/. Archive gate refuses stale lifecycle state. Key files: extensions/openspec/. See docs/openspec.md

SlashCommandBridge enables agent-callable slash commands with typed results, side-effect classification, and confirmation gating. Only /assess fully bridged; /opsx:* available via openspec_manage tool. Key files: extensions/lib/slash-command-bridge.ts, extensions/cleave/bridge.ts. See docs/slash-command-bridge.md

View extension renders files inline (images, PDFs, docs, diagrams, code) with scale controls (/view file large|full|2x) and /zoom fullscreen overlay. Context-aware OSC 8 URIs route to mdserve/editor/file based on file type. Key files: extensions/view/. See docs/view.md

Render extension provides D2 diagrams (render_diagram tool), FLUX.1 image generation on Apple Silicon (generate_image_local tool), Excalidraw rendering (render_excalidraw tool), and native SVG diagramming. Output saved to ~/.pi/visuals/. Key files: extensions/render/. See docs/render.md

Secrets extension manages API keys via clipboard-based input (pbpaste/xclip/xsel/wl-paste), 1Password references (op://...), or shell command evaluation ($(...)). Values never displayed on screen. Key files: extensions/00-secrets/index.ts. See docs/secrets.md

Local inference subsystem manages Ollama for on-device models: manage_ollama, ask_local_model, list_local_models tools. Offline driver switches cloud→local on connectivity failure. Auto-selects best available model (Nemotron/Devstral/Qwen3). Key files: extensions/local-inference/, extensions/offline-driver.ts. See docs/local-inference.md

Tool profiles control which tools are visible in agent context. Built-in profiles (coding, research) reduce prompt bloat. manage_tools tool for agent self-management. Key files: extensions/tool-profile/. See docs/tool-profiles.md

Prompt-template commands (prompts/*.md): /init (project orientation + memory bootstrap), /new-repo (scaffold new repository), /oci-login (container registry auth), /status (project status overview). These are commands — same as registerCommand but defined as markdown prompt templates.

The web-ui extension serves a localhost-only read-only control-plane dashboard: `extensions/web-ui/` exposes a versioned `ControlPlaneState` snapshot and slice routes over HTTP, backed by sharedState plus on-demand design-tree/OpenSpec scans.

Lifecycle state normalization is now implemented via a canonical OpenSpec lifecycle resolver in `extensions/openspec/spec.ts`; OpenSpec status/archive surfaces and design-tree/dashboard lifecycle metadata consume the shared summary instead of independently re-deriving overlapping readiness and binding truth.

Dashboard publisher consolidation is implemented via shared refresh seams: `extensions/openspec/index.ts` routes dashboard publication through `emitOpenSpecState(...)`, and `extensions/design-tree/index.ts` centralizes repeated publisher calls behind `emitCurrentState()` while preserving focus-aware design-tree state in `extensions/design-tree/dashboard-state.ts`.

The web UI control-plane snapshot exposes dashboard.operatorMetadata (effort/model/thinking/memory-oriented state) and recovery.actionable so web consumers can mirror the cleaned-up raised dashboard without parsing footer text. See extensions/web-ui/state.ts and extensions/web-ui/types.ts.

project-memory structural pruning ceiling: computeConfidence() caps effective half-life at MAX_HALF_LIFE_DAYS=90d regardless of reinforcement count. Per-section LLM archival pass runs fire-and-forget at session_start when any section exceeds 60 facts (skips Recent Work). New helpers: FactStore.getSectionCounts(), getFactsBySection(); runSectionPruningPass() in extraction-v2.ts. Commit 25f8076.

Memory system schema v3 (factstore.ts): adds `decay_profile TEXT`, `version INTEGER` (Lamport clock), `last_accessed TEXT` columns to facts table; `embedding_metadata` table for model+dims versioning; `model_name` FK on facts_vec and episodes_vec. Migration in `runMigrations()`. See commit 21b49d1.

api-types.ts is the canonical Omega /api/memory/* wire protocol — defines all request/response types for the TS↔Rust boundary. Includes FactRecord, EpisodeRecord, EdgeRecord, JsonlRecord, EmbeddingMetadata, ContextRequest/Response, RecallRequest/Response, ExtractionAction. Field names are snake_case matching Rust serde conventions. See extensions/project-memory/api-types.ts.

pi ExtensionAPI.registerToolRenderer(toolName, {renderCall?, renderResult?}) — attaches custom renderers to existing tools (including built-in bash/read/edit/write) without replacing the tool. Added in vendor/pi-mono commit 0965ae87. Standalone renderers are stored in Extension.toolRenderers map, looked up via ExtensionRunner.getToolRenderers(), and merged in interactive-mode's getRegisteredToolDefinition().

Omegon is publishable to npm as `omegon` (unscoped). `npm install -g omegon` resolves @cwilson613/pi-{coding-agent,ai,tui} from npm registry. bin/pi uses dual-mode: vendor/pi-mono if present (dev), node_modules/ fallback (installed). Package ~920KB, 149 files. CI auto-publishes on push to main via .github/workflows/publish.yml. NPM_TOKEN secret required.

omegon repo lives at github.com/styrene-lab/omegon (transferred from cwilson613/omegon, old URLs auto-redirect). npm package is unscoped `omegon`. version-check.ts REPO_OWNER is `styrene-lab`. CI auto-publishes on push to main via OIDC trusted publishing.

pi built-in model registry has 23 providers and 792 models. omegon routing only matches 3 (anthropic, openai, local) — 731 models across 20 providers are invisible to tier routing. github-copilot alone has 23 models spanning claude/gpt/gemini/grok. Critical fix needed: use getAvailable() instead of getAll(), and extend tier matching beyond anthropic/openai. See docs/model-degradation.md.

Model routing uses provider-transparent TIER_RULES in extensions/lib/model-routing.ts: declarative pattern table mapping model ID prefixes/exact matches to tiers (gloriana/victory/retribution) with weight, maxThinking, source metadata. matchTierUniversal() matches across ALL providers — not just anthropic/openai. classifyModelTier() exported for /models visualization. getDefaultCapabilityProfile() auto-discovers candidates from all auth'd providers. ProviderName widened to 11+ known providers. Default providerOrder: anthropic > openai > github-copilot > google > xai > groq > mistral > bedrock > azure > openrouter > local.

Provider summary at startup: buildProviderSummary() in extensions/lib/model-routing.ts compares getAll() vs getViableModels() to show tier status. Effort extension displays at session_start: silent when all 3 tiers operational, shows ●/◐/○ per-tier status when degraded. /providers command gives full detail with auth'd/unauth'd providers, tier table, and candidate lists. Level 0 (no providers) warns to run /bootstrap.

Effort system has a tier degradation cascade: when the preferred tier (e.g. victory) has no candidates, it tries adjacent tiers (victory→gloriana→retribution) before falling back to pi's startup default model. See extensions/effort/index.ts session_start handler.

pi-mono npm publish pipeline: package.json uses `file:` refs for local dev (symlinks). CI runs `scripts/publish-pi-mono.sh` which (1) publishes any fork package with a version not yet on npm, (2) rewrites file: refs to pinned registry versions, then omegon publishes with resolved deps. See `.github/workflows/publish.yml` and `scripts/publish-pi-mono.sh`.

pi-mono fork packages use `file:` deps in omegon's package.json (symlinks for local dev). CI publishes via `scripts/publish-pi-mono.sh` which (1) rewrites internal file: refs to pinned versions, (2) publishes ai→tui→agent-core→coding-agent in order, (3) rewrites omegon's file: refs to registry versions. Internal cross-deps also use file: refs so npm resolves them locally. See `.github/workflows/publish.yml`.

Post-v0.58.1 rebase overlap assessment: Most Omegon extensions are NOT obsolete — they build on top of now-first-class pi APIs (custom footer, programmatic compaction, custom providers, packages). Keep: auto-compact (policy layer), offline-driver (routing policy), local-inference (delegation/lifecycle), design-tree branch polling (core doesn't expose general branch-change subscription). Fixed: title ownership (terminal-title.ts sole owner, Ω prefix). Tracked: dashboard footer dedup (seed node dashboard-footer-dedup, priority 4).

/update command (extensions/bootstrap/index.ts): Detects dev mode (.git exists) vs installed mode (no .git). Dev: git pull → submodule update → npm run build → npm install → clear jiti cache → reload. Installed: npm install -g omegon@latest → cache clear → advise restart. Both support --dry-run. /refresh kept as lightweight alias (cache clear + reload only). Replaces old /update-pi.

`/update` in `extensions/bootstrap/index.ts` now implements singular-package lifecycle parity up to restart handoff: dev mode does pull → submodule sync → build → dependency refresh → `npm link --force` → active `pi` verification → cache clear → restart prompt; installed mode does `npm install -g omegon@latest` → active `pi` verification → cache clear → restart prompt. `/refresh` remains cache-clear + reload only.

Internal recursive subprocesses now resolve the Omegon-owned executable explicitly via `extensions/lib/omegon-subprocess.ts` (`process.execPath` + `bin/omegon.mjs`) instead of spawning bare `pi`; wired into cleave child dispatch, bridged assess subprocesses, and project-memory subprocess fallback.
The Rust cleave orchestrator handles child dispatch, worktree management, and merge.
Tests run via `npx tsx --test extensions/*.test.ts extensions/**/*.test.ts`. Root-level glob required — `**` does not match top-level files. No build step needed.
pi footer.render() returns 2 lines (pwd+branch line and stats line). setFooter() replaces it entirely; custom footer must handle all display itself.
shift+ctrl+d is hardcoded in pi-tui tui.js as the global debug key (onDebug). No extension shortcut can override it. See /opt/homebrew/.../pi-tui/dist/tui.js:312.

`npx tsc --noEmit` must exit 0 before committing TypeScript changes. tsconfig.json uses strict:true, Bundler moduleResolution, allowImportingTsExtensions. Scripts: `npm run typecheck` (tsc only), `npm run check` (tsc + tests). Baseline: 0 errors, 56 `as any` occurrences (12 at SDK boundaries). [2026-03-07]

Models with contextWindow < 32768 (mistral:7b, mistral-small, codestral:22b at ~32K) may struggle with deep agent sessions where system prompt alone consumes ~8K tokens. phi4:14b excluded from lists due to 16K context limit.

Dashboard overlay content width limiting is not feasible: pi-tui overlays are composited on top of the terminal buffer at full width. Restricting conversation text wrap to the non-panel area would require a `setReservedColumns()` API in pi-tui's chat renderer — no such API exists. See non-capturing-dashboard design node for full assessment.
