User directive: pi-kit development is inherently spec-and-test-driven. Spec → tests → code pipeline. Cleave + OpenSpec must be fully integrated into all development patterns and skills as the default workflow. Assessment in progress.

Development Methodology directive added to config/AGENTS.md — spec-first is mandatory for cleave_assess complexity ≥ 2.0. Specs define what must be true; code is implementation detail. When specs and code disagree, spec is right.

API contract directive added to config/AGENTS.md: OpenAPI 3.1 spec must be derived from OpenSpec scenarios during Plan phase, placed at openspec/changes/<id>/api.yaml, before implementation begins. Given→preconditions, When→endpoints, Then→responses.
Planned: extend /assess spec (cleave/openspec.ts) to read api.yaml and include endpoint/schema checks in verification checklist — ~50 lines, option 1 of 3 for API contract enforcement.

Runtime OpenAPI validation middleware: pi-kit will prompt user at project-start/major-design-start whether to add middleware validation; guidance will be implementation-agnostic (mention express-openapi-validator, fastify schema compilation) with no language/framework position taken.

`/assess spec` extended to include full API contract validation: injects api.yaml into LLM prompt with endpoint/schema/status-code checks; formatSpecVerification adds contract conformance checklist. See extensions/cleave/index.ts.
pi-kit repo is public at https://github.com/cwilson613/pi-kit with branch protection on main requiring PR reviews.
GitHub main branch protection enabled: 1 approving review required, stale reviews dismissed, force pushes blocked, enforce_admins=false (owner can still push directly).

Root cause of cleave child spec gaps: taskGroupsToChildPlans splits by file scope; cross-cutting scenarios (e.g., RBAC gating) that span multiple layers fall between task groups with no child owning them.

Scenario-first task generation fix: (1) change /opsx:ff skill instructions to group tasks by spec domain not file layer; (2) auto-inject orphan scenarios into best-matching child with ⚠️ CROSS-CUTTING marker; (3) tasks.md group headers annotated with <!-- specs: domain/name --> for deterministic matching. Design node at design/scenario-first-task-generation.md (status: decided).

Scenario-first task generation OpenSpec change scaffolded at openspec/changes/scenario-first-task-generation/ with proposal.md, design.md, tasks.md, and 2 spec files (cleave/spec.md, openspec-skill/spec.md). Committed fc836e7.

Scenario-first task generation being implemented: matchScenariosToChildren() (exported) in workspace.ts performs 3-tier matching (annotation→scope→word-overlap) and auto-injects orphans with crossCutting=true flag. buildDesignSection now accepts pre-computed AssignedScenario[]. See extensions/cleave/workspace.ts.

matchScenariosToChildren 3-tier matching implemented and committed: tier-1 annotation (specDomains), tier-2 scope file reference, tier-3 word overlap; orphans auto-injected with crossCutting=true into best-matching child (scope→word-overlap→last child fallback). See extensions/cleave/workspace.ts.
pi-kit version bumped to 0.1.2; v0.1.2 GitHub release published with version-check extension. See CHANGELOG.md.

Tiered execution loop for cleave children: opus/thinking for plan+review phases, cheap models (haiku/sonnet/local) for execution, iterate review→fix with thinking model up to configurable max iterations. Cost ~50% vs all-opus even with 2 review cycles. D4 promoted from deferred in design/skill-aware-child-dispatch.md.

Skill-to-scope auto-matching uses extensible SkillMapping[] with glob patterns (*.py→python, Containerfile→oci, *.rs→rust, *.yaml→k8s-operations); annotation override via <!-- skills: ... --> in tasks.md; both parsed by extended parseTasksFile. See design/skill-aware-child-dispatch.md.

Skill-aware child dispatch design node at design/skill-aware-child-dispatch.md (status: decided): hybrid scope-annotation matching, directive-style injection, ChildPlan.skills: string[], tiered execution loop (D4) with severity-gated escalation (D4a), diminishing returns guardrail (D4b), adversarial review agent (D4c). See design/skill-aware-child-dispatch.md.

Tiered execution loop review phase uses adversarial /assess cleave posture: severity categories C/W/N gate iteration behavior; critical+security → immediate escalation; >50% issue reappearance between rounds → bail (churn detection); fix agent receives only review issues, not original task. See design/skill-aware-child-dispatch.md D4a/D4b/D4c.
Cleave+design-tree+openspec unified live sidebar investigation in progress: exploring pi overlay system for a persistent collapsible side-panel showing all three extension states simultaneously.

Recommended dashboard architecture: Option B — compact always-on setWidget summary (~3-5 lines) + Ctrl+Shift+D toggle overlay for interactive navigation. Widget updates live via tool_execution_update; sharedState for cross-extension data flow.
Skill-aware child dispatch design complete (status: decided). All Q1-Q8 closed. D1-D6 decided. See design/skill-aware-child-dispatch.md.
Skill-aware dispatch Q7 decided: review runs in same worktree (needs file context, not just diff). Q8: reviews parallelize across children. Q3/Q5: deferred. Q4: composable (multi-skill per child).
skill-aware-dispatch OpenSpec change fully marked complete (all 31 tasks checked off) at openspec/changes/skill-aware-dispatch/tasks.md — commit bc56746.

Unified dashboard D2 decided: footer raise/lower + overlay, no Zellij/tmux — three layers within pi (compact footer always, raised footer on toggle, interactive overlay on-demand). External pane deferred as optional future enhancement. See docs/unified-dashboard.md.

Unified dashboard D1 decided: decentralized emitters + custom footer panel — each extension emits in-memory state to sharedState + pi.events.emit('dashboard:update'); new dashboard extension renders custom footer via setFooter() with raise/lower. Absorbs status-bar.ts. See docs/unified-dashboard.md.

sharedState contract for unified dashboard: designTree (nodeCount/decidedCount/focusedNode), openspec (changes[]{name,stage,tasksDone,tasksTotal}), cleave (status enum + children[]{label,status,elapsed}). See docs/unified-dashboard.md Q1 research.

Unified dashboard D4 decided: live cleave progress via sharedState mutation from dispatcher spawn callbacks (~10 lines); pi.events.emit('dashboard:update') on each child state transition. No file-based IPC needed. See docs/unified-dashboard.md.
Unified dashboard D3 decided: Ctrl+Shift+D for raise/lower toggle (unbound, mnemonic). From raised mode, Enter opens interactive overlay (Layer 2). See docs/unified-dashboard.md.

Unified dashboard D5 decided: raise/lower IS the collapse; state persisted via pi.appendEntry('dashboard-state', {raised}), restored on session_start. Design-tree stops owning its own widget. /dashboard command provides explicit toggle. See docs/unified-dashboard.md.
unified-dashboard OpenSpec change archived to archive/2026-03-07-unified-dashboard; baseline specs merged. All 30 tasks complete (commit 2f14c20).
unified-dashboard tasks.md: all 35 tasks marked done including smoke test (task 7.4: jiti load + mock render verified compact 3-line and raised 9-line modes). Change archived.

Design tree lifecycle D3 decided: convention-based auto-association + OpenSpec archive gate. Branch association is ambient (segment matching on branch name, longest match wins, zero user prompts). `implemented` status set on OpenSpec archive. See docs/design-tree-lifecycle.md.

Lean Astro vault viewer approach: extract remark-wikilinks.ts (~10 lines of generalization to make scan dirs configurable), minimal Astro config, no Starlight or external UI framework. Only deps needed: astro, unist-util-visit, optionally pagefind and force-graph CDN for search/graph.

Obsidian vault-overlay concept being replaced by a lean Astro-based viewport: reuse styrene's remark-wikilinks plugin + minimal Astro config pointed at .pi/ and openspec/ dirs. /vault serve scaffolds Astro site and runs astro dev with hot-reload. No Obsidian dependency.

Viewport options assessed: Obsidian (proprietary), styrene Astro stack (reuse candidate — already has wikilinks, graph, search), Rust CLI viewer (overkill). Decision direction: extract styrene remark-wikilinks plugin into reusable template; add /vault serve command to pi-kit extension.

markdown-viewport design node at docs/markdown-viewport.md: status decided. D1: standalone tool (mdserve fork), pi-kit invokes it. D2: configurable roots, default=project root. Fork at ~/workspace/ai/mdserve (cwilson613/mdserve).

mdserve fork chosen for markdown-viewport: cwilson613/mdserve forked from jfernandez/mdserve. Rust/Axum, Cargo.toml at ~/workspace/ai/mdserve/Cargo.toml. Wikilinks support gap still to be implemented in fork.

markdown-viewport design node decided: standalone Rust binary (fork of mdserve), configurable roots defaulting to project root, pi-kit gets skill (wikilink conventions) + optional /vault serve extension. See docs/markdown-viewport.md.

pi packages install to ~/.pi/agent/git/ (git) or global npm; -l flag installs locally to .pi/git/ or .pi/npm/. Auto-discovers extensions/skills/prompts/themes from conventional directories if no pi manifest.

User directive: implement a generalized bootstrap/first-time-setup system for pi-kit (not just vault/mdserve) — covers ALL external dependencies (Rust/cargo, Ollama, git, node, etc.) with a /bootstrap command and first-run onboarding flow.
Bootstrap first-run detection: existsSync(~/.pi/agent/pi-kit-bootstrap-done) + version string check. MARKER_VERSION constant bumped to re-trigger onboarding when new core deps are added.

User wants /init prompt (first-session env check, mind init, project summary + where-to-begin) and a lighter session-start orientation prompt showing open issues/designs/decisions. To be implemented in pi-kit prompts/ as markdown files. Prompts dir exists at prompts/ alongside new-repo.md and oci-login.md.

Session-start orientation prompt (lighter than /init) should read open designs/decisions from design-tree, open OpenSpec changes from openspec extension, and surface any active cleave jobs — leveraging state already loaded by extensions on session_start.

Centralized dep registry pattern: deps.ts is single source of truth for all external binary deps in pi-kit. Extensions import dep.check() instead of duplicating hasCmd() calls. MARKER_VERSION versioning re-triggers bootstrap when new core deps added.

prompts/init.md (/init) and prompts/status.md (/status) created for first-session orientation and returning-session dashboard respectively. These plus /exit form a session lifecycle (init→status→exit).
User wants /ollama start (or similar) command in pi-kit to launch the Ollama background process from within a pi session — investigation underway via local-inference extension.

pi-extensions skill file identified as critical missing skill for cleave child processes — covers registerCommand API, event names (session_shutdown), ctx shape, confirm() arity. Single highest-impact skill to prevent systematic API-call bugs in extension development tasks.

pi-extensions skill identified as highest-priority new skill: covers registerCommand signature, event names, ctx shape, confirm() arity — prevents systematic API-call bugs in cleave children working on extension code. Now implemented at skills/pi-extensions/SKILL.md.

Module duplication via `.js`/`.ts` import mismatch was DISPROVEN as root cause for dashboard empty-at-startup. Pi's jiti fork uses `moduleCache: false` per extension, but Node's native require cache ensures the same singleton `sharedState` object. Both `.js` and `.ts` specifiers resolve to the same cache key. The `.ts`-only convention was kept for hygiene.

smart-tool-profiles D2 decided: auto-detect profiles at session_start from project signals, with .pi/profile.json override and /profile command for mid-session switching. Proposed profiles: core (always), coding (.git), visual (images/d2), local-ai (ollama), web (always), scribe (MCP configured), pi-dev (pi.extensions in package.json).

smart-tool-profiles D1 decided: tool activation via pi.setActiveTools() is the primary runtime lever; agent can self-serve by enabling tools on demand when user asks. Skills are self-selecting (glob-match); extensions always load but their tools can be toggled.

smart-tool-profiles D3 decided: dedicated manage_tools tool for agent self-serve — enables/disables tools via pi.setActiveTools() with actions: list, enable, disable, profiles, apply_profile. Agent calls it when user requests a capability that may be disabled.

Plan to fix /exit lag: (1) move episode generation before 'Goodbye!' message, (2) replace pi subprocess with direct Ollama HTTP fetch in generateEpisode, (3) parallelize episode embed + JSONL export + MCP close with Promise.allSettled, (4) fire-and-forget fallback if still slow.

/exit lag fix: episode generation moved before 'Goodbye!' message using generateEpisodeDirect (direct Ollama HTTP fetch, ~500ms) with generateEpisode subprocess fallback; exitEpisodeDone flag skips episode in session_shutdown fast path; non-/exit shutdowns use direct Ollama only. Commit d2384ba. See extensions/project-memory/index.ts + extraction-v2.ts

Proposed guardrail priority: G1 (add tsconfig.json + tsc --noEmit) is highest-leverage single fix; G2/G5 (strict mock context smoke tests) are complementary; G3 (runtime Proxy) requires pi-core change. Decision pending.
G1 guardrail decided: tsconfig.json created with Bundler moduleResolution + strict — serves as static typecheck gate matching jiti runtime behavior. tsc --noEmit is the primary type safety mechanism.

extension-type-safety design node decided: tsconfig.json (Bundler/strict), npm typecheck+check scripts, fix all 154 errors for green baseline, then mandate tsc --noEmit in AGENTS.md. Update typescript skill with runtime-only TS anti-pattern. Pre-commit hook rejected in favor of CI gate. See docs/extension-type-safety.md.

User directive: deterministic guardrails (tsc, future linters) must be baked into every phase of the feature lifecycle — authoring, cleave child task files, post-merge, /assess, /opsx:verify — not just pre-commit. This is defense-in-depth for the 'new world' of agentic development.

Guardrail integration priority order: Tier 1 (I1 child task injection + I2 review loop pre-check + I4 /assess preamble, ~20-30 lines each), Tier 2 (I3 post-merge verify + I6 session-start health check), Tier 3 (registry schema, I5 /assess spec, I7 commit reinforcement).

Deterministic guardrails D1 decided: advisory with escalation — guardrail output injected into prompts/reports, failures flagged as Critical-tier, never hard-blocking. Revisit if advisory proves insufficient. See docs/deterministic-guardrails.md.

Deterministic guardrails D2 decided: skill-associated guardrails with condition-based activation — guardrails declared per-skill in SKILL.md frontmatter with condition field (file_exists check); also auto-discover from package.json scripts (typecheck/lint). No separate registry file — skills ARE the registry. See docs/deterministic-guardrails.md.

Deterministic guardrails D3 decided: hardcode tsc+tests for pi-kit now, generalize later. Tier 1 (I1 child injection + I2 review pre-check + I4 assess preamble) first, ~70 lines total. See docs/deterministic-guardrails.md.

Deterministic guardrails D4 decided: I2 review pre-check augments (not replaces) opus review — tsc errors injected as ground truth before reviewer spawns; both static and logic analysis run. See docs/deterministic-guardrails.md.

Deterministic guardrails integration points: I1 (workspace.ts child task file injection), I2 (review.ts pre-check before opus), I3 (post-merge guardrail suite in cleave report), I4 (/assess preamble), I6 (session-start background health check via dashboard). All 5 shipping in one change.

User directive: when agent can perform an action itself (open a file, run a command), it must do so directly — never instruct the operator to perform menial tasks. Operator interaction reserved for decisions and judgment. Enshrined in ~/.pi/agent/AGENTS.md Interaction Model section.

OSC 8 URI scheme landscape: obsidian://open?vault=name&file=path, vscode://file/path:line, cursor://file/path:line, zed://file/path all supported. Excalidraw has no native URI scheme (use obsidian:// via plugin). All schemes optional — file:// is always the fallback.
context-aware-uri D2: editor preference (vscode/cursor/zed) and URI overrides stored in project-local .pi/config.json — same location as tool profiles; not coupled to scribe user_preferences.

context-aware-uri D1: mdserve auto-starts on session_start if binary is on $PATH; all .md/.markdown OSC 8 links route to http://localhost:PORT/path; /vault becomes status/config only; falls back to file:// if mdserve not installed.
context-aware-uri D3: smooth mdserve install — when binary not on $PATH agent offers install via cargo or prebuilt binary; frictionless operator-approval flow.

context-aware-uri cleave complete: vault auto-starts mdserve, view/index.ts wires getMdservePort() (from vault) into resolveUri() for OSC 8 links. Committed on feature/deterministic-guardrails.

Dashboard keybind changed from ctrl+shift+b to ctrl+` (backtick). ctrl+shift+b was intercepted by Kitty terminal as move_window_backward. ctrl+` is free in Kitty defaults but requires Kitty keyboard protocol (no legacy fallback). /dashboard command is the universal fallback. Super/meta keys not supported by pi-tui (only ctrl/shift/alt parsed).

Local model upgrade plan: daily driver (sonnet-tier) → Qwen3 32B Q8, deep reasoning (opus-tier) → Qwen2.5 72B Q5_K_M, cleave leaf/child tasks → Qwen2.5-Coder 32B Q8. Single highest-leverage change: upgrade offline driver from Qwen3 30B → Qwen3 32B Q8.

Hardware-aware model selection strategy: PREFERRED_ORDER is quality-ordered so installed models auto-select best fit — 64GB runs 72B/70B, 32GB runs 32B, 24GB runs 14B Q8, 16GB runs 8B Q8, 8GB runs 4B. Users install what fits; preference list handles routing without per-machine config.

Local model tier hierarchy: 70B (qwen2.5:72b, llama3.3:70b) for deep reasoning on 64GB+; 32B (qwen3:32b, qwen2.5-coder:32b) as primary driver/code tier on 32GB+; 14B (qwen3:14b, qwen2.5-coder:14b) for 16-24GB systems; 8B (qwen3:8b, qwen2.5-coder:7b) for 8-16GB; 4B last useful tier. offline-driver.ts PREFERRED_ORDER covers all tiers automatically.

PREFERRED_ORDER updated to quality-first 70B→32B→30B→22-27B→14B→8B→4B→sub-3B cascade. PREFERRED_ORDER_CODE added with Coder-variant preference at each size tier. Both arrays exported from offline-driver.ts. Old fallback chain (nemotron/devstral/qwen3:30b) retained within the list for graceful degradation.

Local model tier finalization expanded to cover full MacBook spectrum: 70B for 64GB+, 32B for 32GB+, 14B for 24GB (M2 Air sweet spot — qwen3:14b Q8 ~14GB fits with headroom), 8B for 16GB, 4B for 8GB. PREFERRED_ORDER and PREFERRED_ORDER_CODE in offline-driver.ts handle selection automatically.

Local model preference lists (PREFERRED_ORDER, PREFERRED_ORDER_CODE, PREFERRED_LOCAL, PREFERRED_CHAT_MODELS) are maintained in git-tracked source files so new model releases can be added via PR. PREFERRED_ORDER_CODE covers Coder variants at each size tier for cleave leaf workers.
qwen3-coder:30b added to PREFERRED_ORDER_CODE ahead of qwen3:30b and devstral variants — MoE architecture makes it the best local code-agent model for 24GB+ systems despite 30B total param count.

Constraints catch structural gaps that scenarios and falsifiability don't cover.. Acceptance criteria written at exploring time, before research begins — The falsifiability conditions and consequence scenarios must be written before the research, not after. Writing them after is confirmation bias by construction — you already know the answer and write scenarios that your answer satisfies. Writing them before forces honest articulation of what would change your mind and what consequences you're committing to examine. This is the core anti-hallucination mechanism. The constraint checklist can be partially filled in during exploration as structural facts are confirmed.. Inspect reachable via /cleave inspect command + global keyboard shortcut — dashboard widget cannot own input — The dashboard is a non-blocking setWidget — it has no keyboard focus, so per-row shortcuts are not wirable. The inspect overlay is a blocking ctx.ui.custom() call. Two trigger paths: (1) /cleave inspect slash command — explicit, always works; (2) a global keyboard shortcut (e.g. Ctrl+Shift+I) registered while a cleave run is active, opens the same overlay. Inside the overlay, arrow keys select a child, Enter drills in, Escape/Q closes. The shortcut is registered on run start and unregistered on completion so it only appears when relevant.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/cleave-child-observability.md, decision: Inspect reachable via /cleave inspect command + global keyboard shortcut — dashboard widget cannot own input

Episode generation uses the compaction fallback chain: Ollama → codex-spark → haiku — Episode generation currently depends on Ollama at shutdown with a 15-second timeout. Episodes have been silent for 6 days as a result. The compaction fallback chain already exists and proven — reuse it. Try local first if available, fall back to codex-spark, then haiku. Same timeout budget as compaction.. Always emit a minimum viable template episode when all models fail — When every model in the fallback chain fails or times out, construct a skeleton episode from raw session telemetry: date, tool call count, files written (from write/edit calls), and topics extracted from tool call arguments. No model required — assembled deterministically. Better than silence. A model pass enriches it if available; the template is the floor not the ceiling.. Session start loads three layers before first message: recent episodes + recency window + Architecture/Decisions core — Current injection is reactive — waits for first user message then semantically queries. Fails for continuation questions that assume context. Solution: at session_start inject (1) last 3 episodes (narrative of what was worked on), (2) top 20 facts by last_reinforced DESC regardless of section (recency window), (3) Architecture + Decisions sections always. This reconstructs "where we were" before the user speaks.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/memory-session-continuity.md, decision: Session start loads three layers before first message: recent episodes + recency window + Architecture/Decisions core

Semantic retrieval on first message augments the startup payload, not replaces it — Proactive structural load (session start) and reactive semantic retrieval (first message) serve different purposes and must both run. Startup payload gives structural context. Semantic retrieval adds task-specific Constraints/Specs/Known Issues on top. Core sections move from [Constraints, Specs] to [Architecture, Decisions] — those are always loaded. Constraints and Specs are retrieved semantically when relevant.. Task-completion facts triggered by write/edit tool calls, not LLM discretion — Tool-call interception is the most reliable trigger — doesn't depend on the LLM deciding to store. Hook write_file and edit tool calls (intentional changes, not reads or greps). Queue a lightweight fact: "Wrote [file] — [purpose]" where purpose is inferred from surrounding context (file path + recent tool call sequence). Fire-and-forget, non-blocking. Filter: only file writes/edits, not bash commands or reads.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/memory-task-completion-facts.md, decision: Task-completion facts triggered by write/edit tool calls, not LLM discretion

Task-completion facts decay in 1–5 days (business week maximum), no reinforcement extension — Task-completion facts are ephemeral session receipts — breadcrumbs, not architecture. They must be gone within a business week regardless of reinforcement. Decay profile: halfLifeDays=2, reinforcementFactor=1.0 (reinforcement does NOT extend half-life — this is the key difference from architectural facts where reinforcement extends lifetime). A fact written Monday is gone by Friday whether or not it was seen again. This prevents task-completion facts from graduating into permanent memory simply by being reinforced.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/memory-task-completion-facts.md, decision: Task-completion facts decay in 1–5 days (business week maximum), no reinforcement extension

Task-completion facts live in a new "Recent Work" section, separate from Architecture/Decisions — Mixing task-completion facts into Architecture would pollute durable knowledge with ephemeral receipts. A dedicated "Recent Work" section with its own decay profile keeps the separation clean. "Recent Work" is always injected at session start (it's part of the proactive payload) and decays structurally via DB job — no extraction agent involvement needed.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/memory-task-completion-facts.md, decision: Task-completion facts live in a new "Recent Work" section, separate from Architecture/Decisions

Cap effective half-life at 90 days regardless of reinforcement count — Current formula: halfLife = halfLifeDays * reinforcementFactor^(n-1). With reinforcement counts up to 119 and any factor > 1.0 this produces years-long half-lives, making decay functionally inert. Fix: clamp the computed halfLife to a maximum of 90 days. Facts that need to live longer must be explicitly pinned via memory_focus. This gives decay teeth without destroying genuinely durable facts — a fact reinforced every session for 3 months is still around, but it has to keep getting reinforced to stay.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/memory-pruning-ceiling.md, decision: Cap effective half-life at 90 days regardless of reinforcement count

Per-section ceiling: when a section exceeds 60 facts, run a targeted LLM archival pass over that section only — A holistic extraction agent can't audit 1273 facts — it only sees the current conversation. A per-section archival pass is tractable: 60 facts fits in context, the agent can reason about what to keep vs archive within that section. Trigger at session_start when section count > 60. Scoped prompt: "here are all 70 Architecture facts, archive the least useful ones to bring the section under 60." Immune: facts in working memory (pinned). This is separate from the confidence-decay mechanism — two independent ceilings.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/memory-pruning-ceiling.md, decision: Per-section ceiling: when a section exceeds 60 facts, run a targeted LLM archival pass over that section only

Episode generation chain is cloud-first, not Ollama-first — Ollama-first was the wrong call for episode generation. Cloud retribution-tier (haiku, codex-spark) models are: (1) always available if the user can run pi at all — no separate install, no model pull, no RAM requirement; (2) substantially more capable for narrative generation than qwen3:30b; (3) cheap — a 500-token session summary on haiku costs ~$0.0001; (4) fast — sub-second API round-trip vs multi-second Ollama inference on consumer hardware. The 6-day episode silence was a direct consequence of Ollama-first. Cloud models should be primary; Ollama is an optional local preference, not a dependency. The principle: use local inference where the call is high-frequency and model quality threshold is low (embeddings). Use cloud where the call is low-frequency, quality matters, and the cost per call is trivial (episodes, extraction). Ollama is not a reliability upgrade — it is an availability downgrade for most users.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/memory-episode-reliability.md, decision: Episode generation chain is cloud-first, not Ollama-first

Emit OSC 10/11 in defaults.ts to anchor terminal fg/bg to Alpharius values — pi-tui appends \x1b[0m (full reset) after every line — hardcoded in applyLineResets(). On non-Kitty terminals with lighter backgrounds this makes resets bleed through. Fix: emit OSC 10 (fg) and OSC 11 (bg) at session_start with Alpharius palette values so the terminal's native defaults match our palette. Restore via OSC 110/111 on session_shutdown. Kitty silently ignores OSC 10/11 (covered by alpharius.conf). Lives in defaults.ts alongside existing theme enforcement logic.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/alpharius-theme.md, decision: Emit OSC 10/11 in defaults.ts to anchor terminal fg/bg to Alpharius values

Dist symlink as the primary dev-loop mechanism for pi-mono changes — The homebrew dist dir is replaced once with a symlink to pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/dist/. After that, `npm run build` in pi-mono is the entire update step — zero copy, zero file list to maintain. Node's ESM resolution correctly finds pi-mono/node_modules/@cwilson613/* (workspace links) by walking up from the symlink's real path. A deploy script (scripts/deploy-pi-dev.sh) sets up the symlink and is idempotent, so any contributor can run it once.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/pi-fork-update-flow.md, decision: Dist symlink as the primary dev-loop mechanism for pi-mono changes

omegon package.json gets a "build:pi" script pointing to pi-mono — A `npm run build:pi` script in omegon's package.json runs `cd ../pi-mono && npm run build` so contributors don't need to know the relative path. Cross-cutting changes become: edit → `npm run build:pi` (if pi-mono changed) → restart pi. Category A changes still need only a restart.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/pi-fork-update-flow.md, decision: omegon package.json gets a "build:pi" script pointing to pi-mono

Standalone mode: vendor/pi-mono submodule + PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR — Supersedes the dist-symlink approach. omegon now ships vendor/pi-mono as a git submodule and provides bin/pi which sets PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR to the omegon root before invoking the submodule's cli.js. This makes omegon fully standalone: single clone, npm install, and pi works with all extensions, themes, and core changes unified. Required two pi-mono patches: loader.ts and package-manager.ts both now check agentDir for a pi manifest before auto-scanning agentDir/extensions/.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/pi-fork-update-flow.md, decision: Standalone mode: vendor/pi-mono submodule + PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR

Each cleave child is a pi+Omega agent unit, not a bare pi subprocess — A bare pi subprocess inherits the full Omegon extension set — dashboard, memory, inference management — none of which are appropriate in a child task context. The pi+Omega pair gives each child a clean, minimal execution environment: pi handles LLM inference, child Omega handles tool execution and workspace management via the auspex bridge. This also enables k8s deployment as a natural pod spec (two containers, one volume).. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/omega.md, decision: Each cleave child is a pi+Omega agent unit, not a bare pi subprocess

WorkspaceBackend trait abstracts local worktrees from k8s pod workspaces — Git worktrees and k8s pod workspaces have the same logical contract (create branch, expose to child, harvest result, merge back, cleanup) but different physical implementations. A trait boundary keeps the orchestration layer backend-agnostic. Local worktree is the default backend; k8s backends (shared PVC, branch clone, git bundle) are swappable without changing the wave planner or dispatcher.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/omega.md, decision: WorkspaceBackend trait abstracts local worktrees from k8s pod workspaces

Omega runs in two modes: server (full daemon) and worker (child execution only) — The server mode is what the auspex bridge connects to — full HTTP API, Dioxus frontend, lifecycle management. The worker mode is what each cleave child runs — no HTTP server, no dashboard, no Dioxus, just the execution engine and a result channel back to the parent Omega. Same binary, different startup path: `omega serve` vs `omega worker --parent-url http://parent:PORT --workspace /path`.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/omega.md, decision: Omega runs in two modes: server (full daemon) and worker (child execution only)

Branch clone is the default k8s workspace backend; PVC is an optional backend for shared-storage clusters — ReadWriteMany PVCs require NFS/Ceph infrastructure not available on typical local k8s (Docker Desktop, k3s, minikube default storage classes are ReadWriteOnce). Branch clone works on any cluster using only a git remote. For local k8s runs without a persistent remote, Omega starts an ephemeral git daemon (RAII-managed via Drop) for the duration of the cleave run. PVC backend is worth implementing for enterprise clusters with shared storage but is not the default.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/omega-workspace-backend.md, decision: Branch clone is the default k8s workspace backend; PVC is an optional backend for shared-storage clusters

Workspace lifecycle and agent dispatch are separate traits — WorkspaceBackend does not know how the agent runs — Current TS worktree.ts conflates filesystem setup with process management. In Rust, the Workspace trait owns branch lifecycle and result harvesting; AgentBackend (LocalProcess | K8sJob) owns how pi+Omega run against that workspace. The wave planner composes both without coupling either. This separation means a local worktree workspace can be paired with a LocalProcess backend (default) or a K8sJob backend running on a local node mount (for testing k8s paths without a full cluster).. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/omega-workspace-backend.md, decision: Workspace lifecycle and agent dispatch are separate traits — WorkspaceBackend does not know how the agent runs

Decay profile stored as an enum column on each fact — not inferred at read time — Currently facts don't record which decay profile they were written with. A Recent Work fact (halfLife=2d) could be decayed with the standard profile (halfLife=60d) and appear artificially alive. Storing `decay_profile: 'standard' | 'global' | 'recent_work'` per fact eliminates this. In Rust this becomes a `#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)] enum DecayProfile` — the DB value must match a known variant or deserialization fails at insert time, not silently at read time.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/omega-memory-backend.md, decision: Decay profile stored as an enum column on each fact — not inferred at read time

Embedding model name and dimension versioned in DB — dimension mismatch is a hard error, not a silent skip — Current code silently skips vectors with mismatched dimensions. This means a model change silently degrades semantic search to zero results with no user signal. In Omega: an `embedding_metadata` table records the active model name and dimension. On search, if the query vector dimension doesn't match the stored model dimension, return an explicit error with guidance to run `omega memory purge-vectors --model old-model-name`. In Rust, dimension mismatch is a typed error variant, not a conditional skip.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/omega-memory-backend.md, decision: Embedding model name and dimension versioned in DB — dimension mismatch is a hard error, not a silent skip

Pure computation core extracted to standalone module before Rust port — computeConfidence, cosineSimilarity, vectorToBlob/blobToVector, contentHash have zero pi/Node/DB dependencies. Extracting them to core.ts (TS) now lets us write tests against them in isolation, then port those exact tests as the Rust test suite. The behavioral spec travels with the code. During the migration window, both TS and Rust implementations run the same tests against the same inputs — behavioral equivalence is verified before the DB layer switches over.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/omega-memory-backend.md, decision: Pure computation core extracted to standalone module before Rust port

Memory JSONL git-sync format stays identical — new fields (decay_profile, version, last_accessed) are additive with serde defaults on import. Lamport timestamp (version field) fixes conflict resolution: higher version wins unconditionally, preventing archived-fact resurrection. No breaking schema change, no conversion tool needed.

Memory vector store: SQLite BLOB + Rust linear scan permanently. No HNSW, no sqlite-vec. At 384 dims, linear scan takes 0.018ms for 1335 facts vs. 100-500ms embedding latency. Break-even at ~7.4M facts. HNSW and sqlite-vec both fail the composite scoring test (similarity × decay-adjusted confidence requires per-fact parameters). LLVM auto-vectorizes f32 slices.

JSONL format stays identical; Lamport version field added as optional with default 0 on import — Vectors are not in the JSONL (DB-local only), so embedding_metadata adds zero JSONL impact. decay_profile is additive with default "standard". The one meaningful addition is `version: u64` (Lamport logical timestamp) which fixes the git-sync conflict resolution bug: when union-merge produces competing mutations (e.g., one machine archives a fact, another reinforces it), higher version wins unconditionally. Without version, the dedup logic resurrects archived facts by comparing reinforcement_count — a correctness bug for multi-machine operators. version defaults to 0 on import from old files; Lamport clock initializes to MAX(version)+1 after each import. No breaking change, no conversion tool.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/omega-memory-backend.md, decision: JSONL format stays identical; Lamport version field added as optional with default 0 on import

SQLite BLOB + Rust linear scan permanently — no HNSW index, no sqlite-vec — At 384 dims, linear scan of 1335 facts takes 0.018ms vs. 100-500ms for Ollama embedding generation — 5,000-25,000x faster than the prerequisite step. Break-even requires ~7.4M facts (384-dim) or ~3.7M facts (768-dim). A prolific operator storing 50 facts/day accumulates 182,500 facts after 10 years; scan time at that scale is 2.5ms. HNSW is the wrong tool: it trades recall for speed at millions of vectors and cannot express our composite scoring function (similarity × decay-adjusted confidence requires per-fact decay parameters, which a pure distance metric cannot encode). sqlite-vec fails the same test and adds a C FFI dependency. LLVM auto-vectorizes Rust f32 slice iteration — we get SIMD for free.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/omega-memory-backend.md, decision: SQLite BLOB + Rust linear scan permanently — no HNSW index, no sqlite-vec

api-types.ts is the canonical migration contract — Rust Axum handlers must satisfy it exactly — extensions/project-memory/api-types.ts defines all /api/memory/* HTTP request/response envelope types. Field names are snake_case to match Rust serde conventions. The Rust structs derive Serialize+Deserialize with field names identical to these TypeScript interfaces. Any deviation is a bug in the Rust port. The file also defines JsonlRecord (JSONL wire format discriminated union), EmbeddingMetadata, and ExtractionAction — the full set of types crossing the TS/Rust boundary.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/omega-memory-backend.md, decision: api-types.ts is the canonical migration contract — Rust Axum handlers must satisfy it exactly

Access reinforcement via last_accessed column — soft decay timer reset on retrieval — The decay model previously only considered time-since-last-reinforced and explicit reinforcement_count. But when memory_recall returns a fact, that's a strong signal of active relevance — the fact shouldn't decay during the current work session. Fix: semanticSearch calls touchFact(id) on returned results, setting last_accessed to now. The confidence computation uses max(last_reinforced, last_accessed) as the effective time anchor. This doesn't increment reinforcement_count (avoids inflating the long-term persistence signal), just resets the decay timer. In Rust: last_accessed: Option<DateTime<Utc>>.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/omega-memory-backend.md, decision: Access reinforcement via last_accessed column — soft decay timer reset on retrieval

Context injection is budget-aware — Omega renders to a max_chars limit with priority ordering — renderForInjection currently always renders up to section caps regardless of available context budget. In the Omega model, GET /api/memory/context accepts max_chars and stops adding facts when the budget would be exceeded, respecting priority ordering: working_memory (pinned) > semantic_hits > recent_architecture_facts. The TS bridge tells Omega 'give me N chars' and receives a pre-measured block. This prevents memory injection from consuming context needed for the task itself.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/omega-memory-backend.md, decision: Context injection is budget-aware — Omega renders to a max_chars limit with priority ordering

Global DB uses advisory file lock for concurrent migration safety — Multiple Omega instances (one per project) share ~/.pi/memory/global.db. If two instances start simultaneously and both find schema version < expected, both attempt migrations — second one fails or corrupts. Fix: acquire advisory fcntl file lock on ~/.pi/memory/global.lock before checking PRAGMA user_version. Released after migration completes. Hold time is milliseconds. Rust: fs2::FileExt::lock_exclusive(). Standard POSIX semantics, works across processes.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/omega-memory-backend.md, decision: Global DB uses advisory file lock for concurrent migration safety

Context injection uses a unified 6-tier priority pipeline with budget awareness (15% of context, floor 4K, ceiling 16K chars). Tiers: T1 working memory → T2 Decisions top-8 → T3 Architecture top-8 → T4 hybrid FTS5+embedding hits via RRF → T5 structural fill (top-3 per section) → T6 recency fill. No separate bulk/semantic codepaths — when embeddings are unavailable, T4 degrades to FTS5-only via hybridSearch. See commit 613c77d.

Hybrid search combines FTS5 keyword search + embedding cosine search via Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF, k=60). FTS5 catches exact identifiers/paths, embeddings catch semantic similarity. Facts in both lists get boosted. hybridSearch() on FactStore degrades to FTS5-only when queryVec is null. See extensions/project-memory/factstore.ts.

Ollama is a first-class EmbeddingProvider ('ollama'). Auto-detected as last fallback in resolveEmbeddingProvider() — always returns a candidate (OLLAMA_HOST ?? localhost:11434/v1), validated by isEmbeddingAvailable() at startup. Zero config required. Fixes silent 5-day outage where 562 facts had no vectors because Voyage/OpenAI keys were unset. See extensions/project-memory/embeddings.ts.

Hybrid FTS5+embedding retrieval via Reciprocal Rank Fusion — Neither FTS5 nor embeddings alone is sufficient. FTS5 has 100% coverage, zero latency, and catches identifiers — but returns 0 results for paraphrased queries (AND mode) or low-precision noise (OR mode). Embeddings catch semantic similarity — but have 73% coverage (broken indexer left 562 facts invisible for 5 days), ~80ms latency, and mediocre quality on short factual text (278 chars avg) with a 0.6B model. RRF merge (k=60) boosts facts appearing in both lists and degrades to FTS5-only when embeddings are unavailable — no codepath split needed.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/omega-memory-backend.md, decision: Hybrid FTS5+embedding retrieval via Reciprocal Rank Fusion

Unified 6-tier priority pipeline replaces dual bulk/semantic injection — The old semantic mode injected ALL Decisions (315 facts, 143K chars, ~36K tokens) because the 'load all Decisions' design was written when there were ~20. The length<30 cap only gated semantic hits, not core facts. Bulk mode capped at 50 facts but had no query relevance. The unified pipeline fixes both: 6 tiers (WM → Decisions top-8 → Arch top-8 → hybrid search → structural fill → recency fill) with a character budget gate (15% of context, floor 4K, ceiling 16K). Priority ordering means highest-value facts always survive budget pressure. When embeddings are down, T4 degrades to FTS5-only — no separate codepath.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/omega-memory-backend.md, decision: Unified 6-tier priority pipeline replaces dual bulk/semantic injection

Publish as `omegon` (unscoped) to npm — User chose unscoped `omegon`. Simpler install command: `npm i -g omegon`.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/omegon-install.md, decision: Publish as `omegon` (unscoped) to npm

omegon npm CI uses OIDC Trusted Publishing (no tokens): Node 24 + npm ≥11.5.1, id-token:write permission, trusted publisher on npmjs.com → styrene-lab/omegon + publish.yml. NODE_AUTH_TOKEN must NOT be set for OIDC to work. See .github/workflows/publish.yml.

OIDC Trusted Publishing for CI (no tokens) — npm granular tokens still require OTP even with "bypass 2FA" in some configurations. Trusted Publishing via GitHub Actions OIDC (Node 24, npm ≥11.5.1) eliminates all token management. Requires id-token:write permission and trusted publisher config on npmjs.com pointing to styrene-lab/omegon + publish.yml.. Repo under styrene-lab GitHub org — Transferred from cwilson613/omegon to styrene-lab/omegon. GitHub auto-redirects old URLs. npm package stays unscoped `omegon`. version-check.ts REPO_OWNER updated to styrene-lab.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/omegon-install.md, decision: Repo under styrene-lab GitHub org

Preinstall script removes conflicting pi packages — scripts/preinstall.sh auto-removes @cwilson613/pi-coding-agent and @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent during global install to prevent EEXIST on the `pi` bin link. Clear messaging about what it does and how to revert. Also registers `omegon` as a conflict-free bin alias.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/omegon-install.md, decision: Preinstall script removes conflicting pi packages

Cross-tier degradation is confirmation-gated on failure — When a tier's candidates all fail (auth error, deprecation, rate limit), the system should propose degrading to the next tier down with operator confirmation: 'Archmagos unavailable (no viable gpt-5.4 or opus-4-6). Use Magos (sonnet-4-6) instead? [y/n]'. This preserves operator agency — a silent downgrade from opus to haiku mid-session would be surprising and potentially produce worse output without the operator knowing why. Automatic degradation is acceptable only for background/internal tasks (extraction, compaction, episode generation) where the operator doesn't directly see the output.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/model-degradation.md, decision: Cross-tier degradation is confirmation-gated on failure

Use getAvailable() not getAll() for tier resolution — The root cause of the 403 loop: we pass `getAll()` (792 models, no auth check) to `resolveTier()`, which happily selects models the user has no credentials for. Must switch to `getAvailable()` (only models where `authStorage.hasAuth(provider)` is true). This alone would have prevented the gpt-4o 403 — if the user only has Anthropic keys, OpenAI models never enter the candidate pool. DEPRECATED_MODELS filtering then acts as a second layer on top of auth-gated discovery.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/model-degradation.md, decision: Use getAvailable() not getAll() for tier resolution

Proactive registry-time filtering with provider-aware deprecation — Deprecated models should be filtered from the available pool BEFORE tier matching, not just skipped during selection. The filter should be provider-aware: gpt-4o is deprecated on both `openai` and `github-copilot`, but github-copilot may retire models on a different schedule than direct OpenAI API. The filtered pool becomes the single source of truth for all routing decisions. This changes the pipeline to: getAvailable() → filterDeprecated() → resolveTier(). The /models visualization should show this filtered view with clear indication of what was removed and why.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/model-degradation.md, decision: Proactive registry-time filtering with provider-aware deprecation

GitHub Copilot treated as first-class provider via universal tier rules — Rather than special-casing github-copilot as a distinct provider type, we made tier matching provider-transparent. TIER_RULES matches model IDs by pattern (prefix/exact) regardless of which provider serves them. This means github-copilot's claude-opus-4-6, gpt-5.4, gemini-3-pro, etc. all naturally land in the correct tiers without any copilot-specific code. This also future-proofs for Google, xAI, Groq, Bedrock, Azure, and any other provider — they all participate in routing automatically if the user has auth configured. ProviderName type widened to include all known providers. Default policy providerOrder includes all providers in preference order: anthropic > openai > github-copilot > google > xai > groq > mistral > bedrock > azure > openrouter > local.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/model-degradation.md, decision: GitHub Copilot treated as first-class provider via universal tier rules

Local models are NOT universal fallback candidates — Most machines don't have useful local inference capacity. Even on Apple Silicon with MLX, local models barely reach useful capability. Local stays only in explicitly configured roles (servitor/servoskull) for embeddings and image generation — never injected as last-resort into archmagos/magos/adept. Degradation between cloud tiers is handled by cross-tier confirmation-gated fallback, not by silently dropping to a 4B local model.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/model-degradation.md, decision: Local models are NOT universal fallback candidates

Startup provider summary shows degraded tiers, silent when all operational — buildProviderSummary() compares getAll() vs getViableModels() to show auth'd/unauth'd providers and per-tier status (operational/degraded/unavailable). At startup: level 0 shows warning to run /bootstrap, levels 1-2 show tier status with ● operational / ◐ degraded / ○ unavailable, level 3 (all tiers operational) is silent. /providers command gives full detail with candidate tables. Tied into auth via getAvailable() — provider summary directly reflects which API keys and OAuth tokens are configured.. Manifest is an index over existing artifacts, not a copy — The manifest points to state that lives in durable systems (memory facts by ID, design node by ID, openspec change by name, git branch by ref). Token budget: minimal — plain summary facts, no formatting overhead — The session resume context should be a tight plaintext block — goals, active work pointers, next steps. No markdown headers, no bullet hierarchies, no decorative structure. Target: under 1K tokens for the resume injection. The existing systems (memory, design tree focus, openspec) already inject their own context — the manifest only needs to bridge the gap between "what those systems know" and "what the operator was doing".. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/omegon-session.md, decision: Token budget: minimal — plain summary facts, no formatting overhead

Session is repo-scoped, not branch-scoped; cleave is one session with child executions — A session is one operator sitting down to work in one repo. Switching branches mid-session doesn't start a new session — the operator is still pursuing the same goal. Cleave spawning 4 worktrees is one session with 4 child executions, not 5 sessions. The manifest records the branch at exit and the commits made, but the session identity is the (repo, timestamp, operator) tuple. Manifest supplements pi conversation — operates at a higher layer — Pi's conversation history is the provider-facing message stream. The session manifest operates above it — it's injected into the conversation as context (like memory facts are) but is not part of the conversation itself. A resumed session starts a fresh pi conversation with the manifest injected as a system message. This means resume works even when switching providers between sessions.. Non-extension utility modules (debug.ts, sci-ui.ts, shared-state.ts, types.d.ts) live in extensions/lib/ to avoid pi auto-discovery. Pi scans extensions/*.ts as extension factories — only actual extensions with factory exports belong at the root level.

Omegon owns pi-mono as a full downstream fork (vendor/pi-mono/). Bugs and missing features in pi core should be fixed directly in the fork — not worked around in the extension layer. Do NOT register renderCall for pi built-in tools (read, edit, write, bash, grep, find, ls) — it causes pi to skip built-in content rendering (diffs, syntax highlighting, streaming output). Only register for custom/extension tools that have no built-in rendering. See extensions/core-renderers.ts.

Verification should confirm that the active operator path is Omegon-owned, that the resolved runtime root is Omegon, and that restart/update instructions tell the operator to relaunch Omegon. Compatibility probing for `pi` may remain as a secondary sanity check during migration, but it cannot define correctness.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/omegon-binary-identity.md, decision: Executable verification and restart handoff must be expressed in terms of Omegon ownership, not merely successful `pi --where` probing

Omegon now owns the canonical executable boundary: package.json promotes `omegon` to `bin/omegon.mjs`, `/update` verifies and instructs restart through `omegon`, and legacy `pi` remains only as a shim that immediately imports the Omegon entrypoint. See bin/omegon.mjs, bin/pi.mjs, and openspec/baseline/runtime/binary-identity.md.

Fork runtime packages should migrate from `@cwilson613/*` to a styrene-lab-controlled npm scope — The current split between `styrene-lab/omegon` and `@cwilson613/pi-*` undermines the same lifecycle-ownership boundary Omegon just established at the executable layer. Moving the fork packages into a styrene-lab-controlled scope aligns repo authority, package authority, trusted publishing, and release responsibility under one owner.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/fork-package-ownership-alignment.md, decision: Fork runtime packages should migrate from `@cwilson613/*` to a styrene-lab-controlled npm scope

The migration should rename the fork packages into a styrene-lab scope rather than only changing repository ownership — Changing GitHub repo ownership alone would still leave npm publishing, trusted publishing, and dependency resolution anchored to `@cwilson613/*`. The true ownership boundary lives in the package names that Omegon installs and publishes against, so the migration must move package names and dependency references into a styrene-lab-controlled scope such as `@styrene-lab/*`.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/fork-package-ownership-alignment.md, decision: The migration should rename the fork packages into a styrene-lab scope rather than only changing repository ownership

Legacy `@cwilson613/*` packages become compatibility debt, not the long-term release boundary — Once the styrene-lab scope exists, Omegon and the forked monorepo should publish and depend on the new package names. Existing `@cwilson613/*` packages may need temporary compatibility handling or deprecation messaging, but they should no longer define the authoritative release path.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/fork-package-ownership-alignment.md, decision: Legacy `@cwilson613/*` packages become compatibility debt, not the long-term release boundary

Trusted publishing for the styrene-lab package boundary is now live: after bootstrapping the new @styrene-lab/pi-* packages on npm and attaching styrene-lab/omegon -> publish.yml as trusted publisher, GitHub Actions run 23115700122 successfully published both the renamed fork packages and omegon without NODE_AUTH_TOKEN fallback.

Installed Omegon now keeps mutable auth/settings/session state under ~/.pi/agent by default while injecting bundled package resources from omegonRoot via CLI flags; bin/omegon.mjs also migrates legacy package-root auth/settings/sessions forward when shared state is absent. See bin/omegon.mjs and openspec/baseline/runtime/auth-state.md.
Omegon 0.6.5 is the first published release containing the auth-state compatibility fix; GitHub Actions publish run 23116819494 succeeded and npm latest for omegon now resolves to 0.6.5.

Internal subprocess launches must resolve the Omegon entrypoint explicitly — Self-containment requires recursive children and helper subprocesses to re-enter the same Omegon-owned runtime boundary regardless of other binaries on PATH. Bare `pi` is acceptable only as a user-facing compatibility alias, not as an internal execution contract. A shared resolver should compute the canonical executable path or command for the current installation and all internal spawn sites should use it.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/omegon-self-recursive-spawn.md, decision: Internal subprocess launches must resolve the Omegon entrypoint explicitly

0.6.6 should be a release-quality stability pass, not just a point fix — The subprocess-boundary correction surfaced adjacent reliability issues in project-memory search and release hygiene. Shipping a stability step that absorbs those findings, re-verifies behavior, and then re-assesses the result is lower risk than releasing a narrowly scoped fix with known follow-up concerns still open.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/stability-step-0-6-6.md, decision: 0.6.6 should be a release-quality stability pass, not just a point fix

Tighten memory injection by default and gate low-value additions — The session-usage spike is most plausibly caused by an over-generous default per-turn memory payload rather than a missing retrieval feature. 0.6.7 should reduce the default injection budget and make structural fill, episodic memory, and global facts more conditional so routine turns carry less repeated context while still preserving high-priority working-memory and directly relevant facts.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/stability-step-0-6-7.md, decision: Tighten memory injection by default and gate low-value additions

Enforce Node 20+ explicitly instead of attempting Node 18 compatibility — The vendored pi-tui dependency already requires Node >=20 and uses /v Unicode regexes that are not parseable on Node 18. The stability fix is to fail early and clearly through package/startup guardrails rather than carrying a bespoke compatibility fork for unsupported runtimes in a patch release.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/ai/omegon/docs/stability-step-0-6-7.md, decision: Enforce Node 20+ explicitly instead of attempting Node 18 compatibility
Cleave /cleave command delegates planning to LLM via sendUserMessage with ask_local_model instruction — if ask_local_model tool is unavailable the workflow stalls silently with no fallback.

Cleave cleave_run merges all completed children sequentially with no conflict resolution between child branches — conflicts between siblings are only detected semantically (conflicts.ts) not resolved; merge failures are reported but execution continues anyway.

Cleave cleave_run tool silently ignores children whose worktree creation failed — they are marked failed but dispatch proceeds with no error surfaced to caller until final report. Failed worktrees mean those children are skipped entirely.
Cleave child process stdout/stderr accumulates unboundedly in memory — long-running children could exhaust memory (dispatcher.ts:118-119).
Cleave has no loadState/resume capability — state is persisted but a crashed mid-dispatch run cannot be resumed.

Cleave truncateHead import from @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent fails under tsx (ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED) — works at runtime via pi's jiti loader but cannot be tested standalone. See extensions/cleave/index.ts.
project-memory linked minds incomplete — marked as "being rebuilt for SQLite store". /memory link command returns warning.
project-memory /memory edit opens editor but can't save changes back — shows "Direct editing not yet supported for SQLite store"

Cleave task decomposition loses cross-cutting spec scenarios when tasks.md groups are split by file/layer scope (models→service→integration) rather than spec domain. RBAC enforcement example: spec defined capability checks in create_session, but task groups split by layer left the enforcement wire-up unassigned to any child.

TaskGroup.specDomains must be accessed defensively (optional chaining / nullish coalescing) because test fixtures and manual callers omit it — openspec.ts uses group.specDomains?.length and (group.specDomains ?? []) spreads. See extensions/cleave/openspec.ts lines ~237,595-597.

pi-kit issue #5 open: `pi update` always resets to origin/HEAD (main), ignoring pinned tags — tag-pinned installs require upstream change to pi-coding-agent's updateGit(). Workaround: version-check extension notifies on new releases.

Cleave child process execution is opaque to the parent TUI — tool_execution_update only fires for the parent tool; child pi subprocesses have no IPC channel back to parent. Live dispatch progress requires file-based IPC or polling worktree status files.
mergeSmallGroups in openspec.ts deduplicates skills via Set but not specDomains — specDomains merge may produce duplicates when two groups share domain annotations.
buildChildPrompt skill detection uses string includes '## Specialist Skills' — a task description containing that phrase would false-positive. See extensions/cleave/dispatcher.ts.

openspec_manage fast_forward generates empty tasks.md when design.md already exists — spec scenarios not parsed into tasks. Symptoms: 'Parsed: 0 section(s), 0 scenario(s)' on add_spec; spec format mismatch with parseSpecContent parser.
planner.ts now defaults both specDomains and skills to [] at normalization — W1 fixed. Previously only specDomains was defaulted; index.ts patched skills post-hoc.

openspec add_spec parser expects ## ADDED/MODIFIED/REMOVED Requirements + ### Requirement: + #### Scenario: heading format; free-form specs (### S1: style) parse as 0 sections/scenarios and produce empty tasks.md on fast_forward. See extensions/openspec/spec.ts:211.

severityGate logic restructured: PASS→accept first, garbled NEEDS_REWORK/REJECT (0 issues)→escalate, nits-only (no criticals/warnings)→accept. Stale PASS_WITH_CONCERNS branch removed. See extensions/cleave/review.ts.

executeWithReview skips review when initial execution fails (exitCode !== 0) — early return added after disabled check in review.ts to avoid wasting opus call on incomplete worktree.

parseReviewResult garbled output now escalates: severityGate returns escalate when status=NEEDS_REWORK/REJECT and zero issues parsed, rather than silently accepting. PASS_WITH_CONCERNS stale branch removed.

openspec fast_forward generates empty tasks.md when spec files use free-form headings (## Requirements / ### R1: style) instead of the required ## ADDED Requirements / ### Requirement: / #### Scenario: format — sections parse as empty, task generation loop produces no output.
PASS_WITH_CONCERNS with zero issues (no criticals/warnings) is accepted silently by severityGate — logically correct but could mask a malformed reviewer response. See extensions/cleave/review.ts.

cleave_run with 5-child plan (1 foundation + 4 dependents) only dispatched 4 children — dashboard-extension was silently dropped. Possibly a max_parallel=4 interaction where foundation child completed in wave 1 and only 3 of 4 dependents were dispatched in wave 2.

sanitizeBranchName() regex requires final [a-zA-Z0-9] meaning single-char names fail AND any name ending in hyphen/slash/dot returns null. 'feature/a' would pass (ends in 'a') but 'a' alone fails. Short segments after slashes are fine as long as last char is alphanumeric.

bootstrap/deps.ts `ollamaReachable()`: curl command executed synchronously via execSync with 2s timeout — this fires on every check() call including at session_start, blocking the event loop momentarily when Ollama is down. Should be async or cached.

vault/index.ts startMdserve(): port conflict detection is absent — if port 3333 is in use, spawn() silently fails. mdserveProcess is set but the process immediately exits; subsequent /vault status shows 'running' when it is not.

mdserve graph.rs build_graph: slug derivation uses `.to_lowercase().replace(' ', "-")` but node IDs in edges come from wikilinks.rs resolve path which may differ — potential node ID mismatch causing disconnected graph edges if filenames have uppercase chars.
vault/index.ts session_end cleanup calls stopMdserve() but if pi exits abruptly (SIGKILL), session_end never fires — mdserve process becomes orphaned. No PID file or process group management.

mdserve wikilinks.rs build_slug_map: last-writer-wins on slug collision means files with same basename always overwrite each other unpredictably depending on iteration order of file_keys slice. No warning emitted on collision.

mdserve app.rs handle_markdown_file_change: new files created during watch (e.g., via editor temp files) trigger rebuild_all but are NOT added to tracked_files — only existing keys are updated. New markdown files added after server start are invisible until restart.

vault/index.ts: mdserve stdout/stderr piped to 'pipe' but never read — output accumulates in the pipe buffer; long-running server with verbose output could eventually block the child process when the buffer fills.

bootstrap/deps.ts hasCmd() uses `which ${cmd}` via execSync with shell interpolation — a dep id containing shell metacharacters (spaces, semicolons) would execute arbitrary shell code. Low risk with current registry but pattern is unsafe.

Raised dashboard mode (/dashboard) left >50% horizontal space unused — fixed via renderRaisedColumns() two-column layout at ≥120 cols (Design Tree + Cleave left, OpenSpec right). See extensions/dashboard/footer.ts.
Commits must be pushed to origin/main before `pi update` can see them — local unpushed commits are invisible to installations. Pattern repeated across multiple sessions.

bootstrap/deps.ts installDeps() prereq check uses require('./deps.js') dynamic import to check globally available deps not in the install list — mixing ESM static imports with CJS require() is fragile. Should use DEPS array directly.
mdserve bundles force-graph.min.js locally (static/js/force-graph.min.js, 177KB) included via include_str! — resolves W5 offline-first concern. FORCE_GRAPH_JS const added to app.rs.
vault/index.ts hasBinary() reverted to local execSync('which mdserve') — decoupled from bootstrap/deps.ts import to avoid hard load-time dependency. W8 resolved but now duplicates the check.
mdserve graph page XSS fixed (C1): project_id escaped via .replace() chain (backslash, single-quote, <, >) before Rust format!() interpolation into JS string literal.
bootstrap/index.ts installDeps() now async via runAsync() using spawn() with 5-minute timeout and SIGTERM on timeout. No longer blocks event loop with execSync. Exit code 124 signals timeout.
mdserve wikilinks.rs had double strip_prefix bug (url.strip_prefix('/') called twice via shadowing) — fixed by binding url_path once and stripping suffix from url_path. See src/wikilinks.rs.
mdserve templates/main.html used window._lsKey global — replaced with module-scoped __pid + _lsKey() closure (no window pollution). Same fix applied to graph page in app.rs.
mdserve graph.rs build_graph had unused slug_map parameter — removed param and updated call site in app.rs. See src/graph.rs.
sanitizeBranchName() had orphaned validateNodeId JSDoc block above it — fixed in 23c9485. sanitizeBranchName now has its own accurate doc; validateNodeId doc restored.

sanitizeBranchName() regex requires minimum 2 chars (the .*[a-zA-Z0-9]$ anchor requires at least one char after the leading [a-zA-Z0-9] start). Single-char branch names like 'a' are rejected. Multi-char names like 'ab' and 'feature' pass correctly.
matchBranchToNode JSDoc fixed to say 'implementing only' — done in 23c9485. No longer claims it matches implemented nodes.

sanitizeBranchName() .lock check regex /\.lock(\/|$)/ only rejects components that end exactly in '.lock' or '.lock/'; 'foo.lock.extra' passes the check (git also allows it — only literal .lock component endings are disallowed).

parseFrontmatter naive YAML parser: kvMatch regex `/(\w[\w_]*):\s*(.*)/` captures entire rest-of-line including semicolons — `branch: feature/foo; rm -rf /` stores the full string. sanitizeBranchName() correctly rejects it (semicolons not in allowlist), so the poisoned-branch test passes for the right reason but the 'poison' is silently read verbatim before validation.

pi-kit working tree clean as of ada9a5e. All prior uncommitted changes (bootstrap/deps.ts, dashboard/footer.ts, design-tree/*, openspec/archive-gate.ts, project-memory/index.ts) were already committed; only vault/index.ts + facts.jsonl remained uncommitted.
pi-kit had uncommitted changes committed and pushed at ada9a5e: vault/index.ts (DEPS import removed, direct binary check restored) + facts.jsonl sync. Working tree now clean.

mdserve all 47 tests passing after XSS fix, offline graph bundling, path traversal test, wikilink rendering test, and build_graph param cleanup (commit 96b8f95 on feature/wikilinks-graph).

local-inference /ollama pull uses stdio:'inherit' — will hang silently if pi's TUI intercepts stdin during the pull. Large model pulls (10-30GB) run synchronously in the terminal with no progress indication to pi's message stream.
manage_ollama 'start' and /ollama start share the same 15s startup wait loop but the tool version doesn't notify the user of progress while waiting — agent will appear hung for up to 15s.
manage_ollama 'pull' action wraps result in a double `.then(r=>r)` promise chain — `resolve(text(...).then(r=>r))` is redundant but harmless.
manage_ollama 'pull' action uses stdio:'pipe' and accumulates all output in a string — large model pulls (10-30GB) produce no streaming feedback to the agent during download.
manage_ollama 'stop' action: pkill -f 'ollama serve' via execSync uses shell interpolation — shell injection risk if Ollama binary path contains metacharacters (low risk but pattern is unsafe).

examples/dashboard-test/ IS a live pi project: package.json registers seed-cleave-state.ts extension. design/ and openspec/changes/ populated. `pi` in that dir loads all extensions and shows a populated dashboard. Committed be0bcc9.
manage_ollama 'pull' action: resolve(text(...).then(r=>r)) double-promise chain is harmless but redundant — confirmed still present in live code.
No tests for manage_ollama tool actions, /ollama command parsing, isOllamaReachable, or startOllamaProcess — four distinct action branches with zero test coverage.

Two session_start handlers in local-inference/index.ts (lines 192 and 758) — first refreshes models (fails if Ollama offline), second auto-starts Ollama. Race: first handler may set serverOnline=false before second starts Ollama. Should be merged into one handler.
local-inference/index.ts text() helper is declared async unnecessarily; pull handler uses resolve(text(...).then(r=>r)) double-promise chain — harmless but messy.

bootstrap/index.ts installDeps() prerequisite check was checking stale array instead of re-running dep.check() — if cargo install failed, mdserve install proceeded anyway. Fixed: now calls DEPS.find(reqId).check() live.

local-inference had two session_start handlers (model refresh + auto-start) with race-condition ordering. Fixed: merged into single handler — refresh first, then auto-start if offline, then refresh again after startup.

pi ExtensionAPI uses registerCommand(name, {handler(args, ctx)}) not addCommand({name, execute(ctx, args)}). Session cleanup event is "session_shutdown" not "session_end". confirm() takes (title, message) — two args. All fixed in commit 9ef1d40. [2026-03-07]

STATUS_ICONS in overlay-data.ts extended with implementing (⟳/warning) and implemented (✓/success) entries — previously fell back to ○ fallback. Fixed 02fa23e. See extensions/dashboard/overlay-data.ts.

overlay-data.test.ts extended with 5 new tests: focused-node dedup (O1), question badge rendering (O2), badge omission when 0 questions, implementing/implemented icon coverage, runId:null→undefined fix. Total: 30 tests passing. Commit 02fa23e.

Dashboard OpenSpec/Cleave missing at session_start: root cause was .js/.ts import mismatch on shared-state — openspec/cleave used ../shared-state.js, dashboard used ../shared-state.ts, creating separate singletons. Fixed in commit 2e43029 by unifying all imports to .ts extension.

All local .ts module imports normalized from .js to .ts across 23 files (59 imports) in commit b6d02fd. jiti caches by resolved path including extension — .js and .ts produce separate module instances. Only @modelcontextprotocol SDK imports retain .js (actual JS package exports).

jiti module cache: `createJiti(cwd)(path+'.ts')` vs `createJiti(cwd)(path+'.js')` produce different module wrapper objects but share the same underlying exports (sharedState identity preserved). The .js/.ts mismatch fix (b6d02fd) is correct in principle but the root isolation was already partially mitigated by jiti's cache internals.

jiti loader.js uses `moduleCache: false` (per-extension new jiti instance) BUT shared-state.ts uses Symbol.for() key on globalThis — globalThis is shared across all jiti instances in the same process. Module objects differ but sharedState singleton is preserved. The .js/.ts root cause theory was WRONG; actual dashboard empty-at-start bug was openspec emitter timing (session_start vs before_agent_start). See dist/core/extensions/loader.js.

setFooter factory is called synchronously in setExtensionFooter (interactive-mode.js:1030) so tui is set before queueMicrotask fires. But if session_start handlers run before TUI is initialized, ctx.ui may be a no-op stub — investigating as root cause for OpenSpec missing at session start.

openspec/dashboard empty-at-start root cause still uncertain: added debug logging (debug() calls in emitOpenSpecState + footer render) to gather evidence. debug() function not yet implemented — needs to be added as shared utility.
All 32 remaining .js imports in test files converted to .ts in commit 10daf2d. Zero .js local imports remain across all extensions and test files.
All .js imports in test files (32 files) converted to .ts in commit 10daf2d — tsx .js→.ts resolution still works but convention now consistent.
test files .js import inconsistency fully resolved in commit 10daf2d — 32 test files converted from .js to .ts local imports. 917/917 tests passing.

executeImplement writes the node to disk as 'implementing' before attempting git branch creation — if git checkout -b fails silently, node is persisted as implementing but branch never exists; no rollback, no test for partial-failure case. See extensions/design-tree/index.ts:149-162.

See extensions/dashboard/footer.ts.
/reload does not apply changes to extensions installed via git (pi update path) — local file edits aren't picked up by /reload; requires `pi update` or full restart to reflect committed changes.

/reload does NOT hot-reload changed module code — jiti's moduleCache:false creates a new jiti instance per extension load, but since reload() just re-imports the same path, Node's native require cache (or jiti's in-process cache) may serve stale compiled modules. Changes require `pi update` or full restart. Confirmed in agent-session.js:1784.
INVALID ID - skip

jiti filesystem cache at $TMPDIR/jiti/ uses path-based hashing, not content-based. Extension source changes are NOT picked up by /reload because stale transpiled .mjs/.cjs files are served from the fs cache. Workaround: `rm -rf $TMPDIR/jiti/` before /reload. Proper fix: pass `fsCache: false` or `rebuildFsCache: true` to createJiti in pi-coding-agent's extensions/loader.js:219.

Plan: clear jiti fsCache on session_shutdown so /reload always picks up fresh source. session_shutdown fires before reload's session_start re-run. Implementing in extensions/defaults.ts or dashboard/index.ts.

jiti fsCache filename hash is path-based (utils_hash(filename)), NOT content-based — stale .mjs/.cjs persist until manually deleted even after source edits. Old entries accumulate. Clear with `rm -rf $TMPDIR/jiti/` before /reload.

ctx.reload() IS available on ExtensionCommandContext (confirmed in types.d.ts line 237 under ExtensionCommandContextActions). /refresh uses ctx.reload() directly — no session.reload() indirection needed.

ctx.ui.notify() SDK signature requires 2 args (message, type:'info'|'warning'|'error'); 'success' is not in the union. 19 call sites use 1 arg or pass 'success' — all type errors. See extensions/bootstrap/index.ts and others.

bootstrap/index.ts shadow CommandContext interface removed — all 35 type errors fixed by importing real SDK types and replacing ctx.say() with ctx.ui.notify(). Part of extension-type-safety cleave.

Every guardrail in pi-kit today is advisory (directive text) or heuristic (AI review). Deterministic oracles (tsc, test runner) are never auto-invoked — no lifecycle phase automatically gates on passing tsc or tests.

Terminal image rendering (view tool) outputs high-res images (e.g., 3816×3060) at native resolution — text and detail become illegible at terminal width. No zoom/scale option exists in pi's current view tool. pi reads the image and sends as base64 attachment; TUI rendering uses terminal image protocol at pixel-to-pixel density.

pi Image component caps rendering via Math.min(width-2, options.maxWidthCells??60) — height derived from aspect ratio, maxHeightCells option has no effect. See /opt/homebrew/.../pi-tui/dist/components/image.js.
